title: Hyperscaler latency matrix & next test plan
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Sanjay Mehta <sanjay.mehta@streamlinehealth.com>
Cc: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-04-18T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Post-demo: hyperscaler latency matrix & proposed tests

Hi Sanjay —

Thanks again for taking the demo yesterday. Quick recap and a proposed, light set of tests so we can benchmark actual customer-shaped traffic across regions:

Summary:
- Your primary concern: end-user latency across US East/West and EU for a mixed chat + embedding workload.
- Competitors evaluated in call: hyperscaler-managed endpoints (H-Opt), Generic LLM API X, and our current hosted offering.

Proposed next steps (small, time-boxed):
1) Run 3 synthetic runs (p95-focused):US-East (us-east-1), US-West (us-west-2), EU (eu-west-1) — 1k short-chat requests + 500 embedding requests each.
2) Compare: p50/p95 latency, token cost, and error rates vs H-Opt across identical model family (open-llm-13b quantized).
3) Capture cold-start behavior and tail latency with a 30m idle window between bursts.

Attachments: I attached a latency matrix (latency-matrix-v1.xlsx) with columns we suggest and a short runbook (runbook-hyperscaler-tests.pdf).

Can you confirm the three regions look good and whether Streamline can run these from your staging VPC (preferred) or if you want Redwood to kick off tests from our dedicated infra? Once you confirm I’ll schedule the runs (estimate 2h total).

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai
+1 415-555-0123



From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-04-18T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Post-demo: hyperscaler latency matrix & proposed tests

Ben —

Quick internal note before we schedule: a couple of things to make the comparison apples-to-apples and surface Redwood differentiators.

Observations / adjustments:
- Match model checkpoints & quantization profile (quantized-8) with H-Opt test images; otherwise kernels differ and skew p95.
- Include a variant that enables KV cache reuse for chat sessions to show our prefix caching benefit (save tokens + reduce latency).
- Add one cost-efficiency metric: tokens/sec per dollar and a short raw-cost column.

Operational asks:
- Neha, can you draft the run command that toggles cache reuse + batching options and note the expected resource footprint?
- Marissa, please prepare a short readout template (single-slide) that captures p50/p95, cold-start, cost, and a one-line recommendation.

I’ll be on the call with Ben when we kickoff.

—Camila
Camila Reyes
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
camila.reyes@redwood.ai



From: Sanjay Mehta <sanjay.mehta@streamlinehealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Alyssa Chen <alyssa.chen@streamlinehealth.com>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-04-18T15:30:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo: hyperscaler latency matrix & proposed tests

Ben —

Thanks for the clear plan. We can run from our staging VPC (preferred) but need Redwood to share the exact test harness and expected egress/IP allowlist. A couple of clarifying Qs from our infra/security: 

1) Data residency: embedding vectors are transient but our compliance team prefers that the embedding hashing step remains in-region. Can the test confirm no external vector persistence?
2) Model variants: we prefer keeping model weights in-region for the EU run (regulatory constraint). Can Redwood run the EU test using a model instance pinned to eu-west-1?

We’re fine with the three regions. If you can provide run commands and an allowlist by EOD tomorrow we’ll queue staging runs for Friday.

Appreciate it —
Sanjay
Director of Platform Engineering
Streamline Health
sanjay.mehta@streamlinehealth.com



From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-04-19T11:45:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Post-demo: hyperscaler latency matrix & proposed tests — run commands & allowlist

All —

Draft run commands and allowlist below for Sanjay (and Alyssa): please sanity-check before I send to customer. I kept flags minimal to reproduce H-Opt runs and one Redwood-optimized run.

1) Baseline (H-Opt parity):
redwood run --model open-llm-13b --quantize q8 --region {REGION} --concurrency 8 --samples 1500 --type mix

2) Redwood-optimized (cache + batching):
redwood run --model open-llm-13b --quantize q8 --region {REGION} --concurrency 8 --samples 1500 --type mix --kv-cache=true --auto-batch=adaptive

Notes:
- Replace {REGION} with us-east-1 / us-west-2 / eu-west-1.
- Cold-start test: run the command, then wait 30m idle, then run a 200-sample burst and capture p99.
- Cost calc: attach token counts produced by run and we’ll compute tokens/sec per dollar in the sheet.

Allowlist (IPs for Redwood test runners): 34.201.23.10, 34.201.23.11

I’ll attach a cleaned latency-matrix next and drop a one-slide template for Marissa to use. Once Ben confirms I’ll send to Sanjay + Alyssa.

-Neha
Neha Kapoor
Product PM, Performance
neha.kapoor@redwood.ai

title: HelixHealth renewal: proposed on-prem expansion & isolated clusters
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Rahul Gupta <r.gupta@helixhealth.com>, Aditi Mehra <procurement@helixhealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 15:02:00 -0700
Subject: Renewal conversation: on-prem expansion at renewal

Hi Rahul / Aditi —

Quick note as we approach the June renewal for HelixHealth: you mentioned considering an on-prem environment split for regulatory isolation across two new clusters (one for analytics, one for patient-facing inference) as part of the renewal. We should scope this into the upcoming contract so delivery can start right after renewal sign.

High-level ask: can you confirm the target scope (number of clusters, any hard isolation/regulatory requirements, and expected go-live window)? From our side we’ll prepare a draft SOW, cost delta, and an updated term proposal (option to extend from 12->36 months with capacity commits to lower unit pricing).

If helpful I can set a 30-min call this week to align stakeholders — propose Wed 4/14 10am PT.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0168

Attachments: helix_request_notes.txt (summary notes)

From: Rahul Gupta <r.gupta@helixhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Aditi Mehra <procurement@helixhealth.com>
Date: Tue, 13 Apr 2027 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal conversation: on-prem expansion at renewal

Thanks Ben — we are definitely interested. A few immediate clarifications: 

- Scope: we want two *physically isolated* clusters (air-gapped network segments within our data centers) with separate VPCs and KMS keys; one cluster sized for sustained low-latency inference (customer-facing), the other for large-batch analytics (nightly jobs).
- Timeline: target production by end of Q3 (Sept 30).
- Compliance: we will need SOC 2 evidence and an inventory of data residency and logging features.
- Commercial: looking at a 36-month committed term if price/SLAs look good.

Please send topology diagrams and an estimated cost delta vs our existing private footprint. We can take a call on Wed — 10am PT works.

—Rahul
Rahul Gupta
CTO, HelixHealth
rahul.gupta@helixhealth.com

On Mon, Apr 12, 2027 at 3:02 PM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> (original message)


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 14 Apr 2027 09:10:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: HelixHealth renewal: on-prem expansion (internal)

Team — forwarding Rahul's note. Key asks: two physically isolated clusters, separate VPCs/KMS, SOC2 artifacts, target prod by 2027-09-30, interested in 36-month term.

Action items I want from the delivery/pricing side before Wed 10am call: 
- Marcus: high-level topology diagram and ops approach for isolation + rough delivery timeline.
- Evelyn: draft SOW (milestones, acceptance criteria, risk items) and resource needs.
- Priya: confirm whether private package supports separate KMS per cluster and any additional runbook items for air-gapped network setup.
- Laura: please prep an initial commercial delta (monthly and committed pricing) and options for 12/24/36 months.

I’ll be on the call with the customer. Please share artifacts to me and I’ll circulate to HelixHealth before the meeting.

Thanks —
Ben

--- Forwarded message attached: helix_request_notes.txt


From: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 15 Apr 2027 11:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: HelixHealth renewal: on-prem expansion (internal)

Thanks Ben — I pulled together a first-pass SOW and timeline. High-level notes below and attached is SOW_Helix_OnPrem_Expansion_v1.pdf plus a network topology (HelixTopology_v2.png).

Summary (draft):
- Scope: provision two isolated clusters in HelixHealth data centers (Cluster-A: low-latency inference; Cluster-B: batch analytics), each in separate VPCs, separate KMS keys, distinct logging sinks.
- Timeline: 12 weeks from contract signature to pilot (Cluster-A limited inference), 20 weeks to full production for both clusters. Buffer for procurement and rack provisioning not included.
- Deliverables: infra buildbook, runbooks, acceptance test suite, 6-month hypercare.
- Preconditions: HelixHealth to provide network racking windows, firewall change windows, and KMS provisioning details.
- Pricing drivers: cluster sizing, desired replication, and committed term. Estimated capex/one-time setup and OPEX delta included in pricing spreadsheet (pricing_delta_breakdown.xlsx).

Risks and notes:
- Air-gap/physical isolation introduces extended lead times for hardware/network approvals; recommend we include explicit procurement lead-time in SOW.
- If they commit to 36 months we can give a tiered discount that materially reduces per-token inference cost.

I’ll iterate once Marcus and Priya confirm the KMS/network details.

Evelyn Hart
Director, Customer Delivery | Redwood Inference


From: Aditi Mehra <procurement@helixhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Rahul Gupta <r.gupta@helixhealth.com>
Cc: procurement-ops@helixhealth.com
Date: Fri, 16 Apr 2027 08:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal conversation: on-prem expansion at renewal

Ben / Rahul — thanks for the context. Procurement needs two things to move: 

1) An updated quote that shows one-time setup fees and ongoing recurring fees (monthly) for 12/24/36 month terms. Please highlight the savings at 36 months.
2) Security/compliance artifacts (we need SOC2 attestation, logging/retention policy, and KMS key handling documentation) to run through our security review.

Also: can you confirm proposed payment terms and any termination clauses if hardware provisioning is delayed? We prefer net 60 but can be flexible with a small discount for net 30.

Please send the commercial package and the SOC2 stub by EOD Mon so we can start internal review.

Regards,
Aditi Mehra
Sr. Procurement Manager | HelixHealth
procurement@helixhealth.com


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Rahul Gupta <r.gupta@helixhealth.com>, Aditi Mehra <procurement@helixhealth.com>
Cc: Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 19 Apr 2027 11:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: HelixHealth renewal: proposed on-prem expansion & isolated clusters

Hi Rahul / Aditi — appreciate the quick turnaround. Attached are the consolidated artifacts for your review: SOW_Helix_OnPrem_Expansion_v1.pdf, HelixTopology_v2.png, pricing_delta_breakdown.xlsx, and a SOC2 attestation stub. Summary of our commercial proposal: 

- Term options: 12/24/36 months (see pricing spreadsheet). 36-month committed term includes a 22% reduction on per-token fees and waived onboarding fees if signed by 2027-06-01.
- One-time setup: covers infra build, runbooks, and on-site validation (detailed in SOW).
- Payment terms: default Net 60; Net 30 available with 1.5% early-payment discount.
- SLA: production latency and availability targets included in SOW; we propose 99.95% uptime for production clusters with standard remedies (credits).

Next steps: 
1) Aditi — if you can confirm receipt and flag any legal/contracting constraints we’ll get a redline-ready contract over to you by EOD Wed.
2) Rahul — if the topology looks good I’ll ask Evelyn to prep a detailed delivery milestone plan for pilot cutover.
3) Internal: Evelyn/Marcus/Priya — please post the final SOW + pricing into the Drive link below and I’ll push the package to DocuSign.

Drive: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3c-helix-expansion
HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/deals/HE-0043
DocuSign (preview): https://www.docusign.com/helixt-2027-onprem

Thanks — we’re aligned to move quickly if you are.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0168

Attachments referenced above: SOW_Helix_OnPrem_Expansion_v1.pdf, HelixTopology_v2.png, pricing_delta_breakdown.xlsx, SOC2_Attestation_stub.pdf

title: Regional node footprint rationalization for Orchard Analytics commitment
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwoodinference.com>, Irene Choi <irene@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>, Jamie Liu <jamie@orchard-analytics.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Regional node footprint rationalization for Orchard Analytics commitment

Team —

We just moved HS-8439 into contract review. Orchard has committed to a 36-month Dedicated capacity contract with a phased ramp: aggressive growth in US+EU, smaller footprint in APAC but want a Singapore PoP for latency. Their traffic forecast is attached (traffic_forecast_q3.csv). Key asks from the AE/CS call:

- EU data residency for inference and logs (must keep EU traffic within eu region)
- EU canary for 2 weeks before full cutover
- Initial committed baseline: 24 A100-80GB-equivalent GPUs across regions; burst to 40 for seasonal spikes
- Support for mixed model strategy (H100 for heavy gen, A100-80 for steady-state)

Can we get a suggested node footprint (per AZ counts), a ramp cadence (weeks 0/4/8/12), and an estimated monthly committed spend to feed back to procurement? Attached is the Orchard MPA checklist they shared.

Also flag any hard constraints for EU-only routing, pre-warm windows, or legal/procurement gating that will slow the timeline. Planning to reply to Jamie by EOD next Thursday.

Thanks —
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben@redwoodinference.com

Attachments: orchard_mpa_requirements.pdf, traffic_forecast_q3.csv


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>, Irene Choi <irene@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 13 Apr 2027 10:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Regional node footprint rationalization for Orchard Analytics commitment

Ben —

High level sizing and constraints below. I scoped for performance (p95 latency targets < 120ms for short-form generation) and cost predictability for a 36-month commit. Assumptions: 70% of traffic originates US, 20% EU, 10% APAC per the forecast. Models: mix of gen+embedding with 40% of tokens from heavy-gen flows.

Proposed committed baseline (equivalent A100-80GB GPUs):
- US (primary): 14 committed (2 AZs, 7 nodes each), burst 24
- EU (primary, GDPR-resident): 7 committed (2 AZs, 4/3 split), burst 12. EU canary = provision mirrored subset (2 nodes) with routing test window
- APAC (singapore edge): 3 committed (single AZ), burst 4

Notes:
- Use H100-backed instances as optional burst class for heavy-gen during predictable windows; treat as metered burst due to cost delta.
- Pre-warm: recommend a 72-hour pre-warm for EU if cold starts may impact p95; US can use 24–48h for predictable events.
- KV cache warmup: for their “sessionized” workload plan, reserve 1.5x KV capacity on EU nodes to avoid cross-region fetches.

Ramp cadence (weeks after contract start):
- Week 0: deploy baseline in US + EU (scaled to 60% of baseline for EU canary subset live)
- Week 2: EU canary start (routing split 10% live traffic). Monitor quality/regression for 14 days.
- Week 4: commit to full EU baseline; APAC baseline live at reduced capacity for testing
- Week 8: burst capacity enabled for US as needed; evaluate H100 bursts
- Week 12: full burst+baseline operational

Estimated committed monthly spend (list price proxy, subject to discounts):
- Baseline (24 A100-eq): ~$295k/mo before enterprise discount
- Expected committed price after HS-8439 discounts: ~$195k–220k/mo

Constraints / blockers:
- EU data residency requires legal signoff on our private logging pipeline; may need Private deployment in EU region or dedicated customer logging bucket with retention controls.
- Procurement: need signed SOW with exact ramp weeks to lock hardware windows; availability windows for H100s are limited in APAC in Q2.

I can put numbers into the Node Map workbook (proposed-Node-Map-v2.xlsx). If you want, I can run a sensitivity for 10–20% higher traffic and show impact to committed vs burst.

— Karthik
Head of Infrastructure, Redwood Inference

Attachments referenced: proposed-Node-Map-v2.xlsx


From: Jamie Liu <jamie@orchard-analytics.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: procurement@orchard-analytics.com
Date: Wed, 14 Apr 2027 08:44:00 +0100
Subject: Re: Regional node footprint rationalization for Orchard Analytics commitment

Ben / team —

Thanks for the quick turnaround. A few clarifications from our side:

1) EU data residency: must ensure tokens/logs for EU-routed requests do not leave EU; we can accept a regional logging endpoint and customer-managed KMS.
2) Canary: EU 2-week canary is mandatory. We will route 5–10% initially and increase only after SLO verification.
3) Cost: our procurement team expects an initial committed monthly spend not to exceed $210k for Year 1 (we can go higher in Y2/Y3 if ROI metrics look good).
4) Can you confirm RTO/RPO for EU (auditable) and whether you support cross-region failover with strict no-log-export to US?

Procurement: procurement@orchard-analytics.com will need a short SOW showing ramp dates + termination clauses. We can do 60-day SLA review after the EU canary.

Jamie Liu
Head of Platform Engineering, Orchard Analytics
jamie@orchard-analytics.com


From: Irene Choi <irene@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>, Jamie Liu <jamie@orchard-analytics.com>, procurement@orchard-analytics.com
Date: Fri, 16 Apr 2027 11:22:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Regional node footprint rationalization for Orchard Analytics commitment

Jamie / Ben / Karthik —

I updated the Node Map workbook with an EU-focused canary plan and an alternate lower-cost configuration that uses more quantized A100-40GB nodes behind the infrequent heavy-gen paths. Summary of changes:

- Revised committed baseline to fit Jamie's Year 1 cap of $210k/mo by shifting 2 A100-eq from committed to metered-burst (H100) and adding more 40GB quantized nodes for embeddings-heavy flows. New baseline equivalent: 22 A100-eq committed (US 13, EU 6, APAC 3).
- EU canary flow: 2 EU warm nodes (routing 10% initially), canary test window 14 days with pre-defined evals (prompt set v1 attached in Drive).
- Data residency: propose customer-managed KMS + EU S3 bucket for logs; cross-region failover is limited to compute-only (no log replication). RTO target for EU failover: 6 mins for compute, RPO for logs: 0 (no automatic replication).
- Legal gating: we need an Appendix for data residency in the SOW.

Action items:
- Ben: confirm Orchard accepts the updated baseline (22 A100-eq) and the canary plan by EOD Tue 2027-04-19.
- Karthik: reserve H100 burst capacity tentative windows for May–June (APAC constraint).
- Marissa: draft SOW appendix for EU data residency and appendix for billing cadence (monthly with quarterly true-up).

I placed a snapshot of the revised cost table and the canary checklist in Drive: https://drive.google.com/open?id=1aB2cDriveDocExample.

Irene Choi
Capacity Planning Lead, Redwood Inference
irene@redwoodinference.com


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jamie Liu <jamie@orchard-analytics.com>, Irene Choi <irene@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: procurement@orchard-analytics.com, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 18 Apr 2027 17:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Regional node footprint rationalization for Orchard Analytics commitment

Jamie —

Thanks everyone for the fast turn. Jamie — per Irene's update we propose the following final commitments for Year 1 under HS-8439:

- Committed baseline: 22 A100-equivalent GPUs (US 13 / EU 6 / APAC 3)
- Burst policy: up to 40 A100-eq via metered H100s during predictable spikes (managed windows)
- EU canary: 2-node warm canary, 14-day window, routing 10% -> 100% after pass criteria (SLOs defined in Drive)
- Data residency: customer-managed KMS + EU logs in EU bucket; no automatic log replication to US; cross-region compute failover only
- Estimated committed monthly cost (post-discount): $205k/mo for Year 1

Next steps: Marissa to send SOW + Appendix for EU data residency; procurement@orchard-analytics.com please confirm acceptance of the $205k/month baseline and the canary window. Karthik will start tentative H100 reservations and Irene will schedule the canary checklist review.

Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben@redwoodinference.com

Attachments referenced: orchard_mpa_requirements.pdf, proposed-Node-Map-v2.xlsx

title: Renewal: reserved GPU increase & updated per-hour terms
preview:
From: Jordan Ellis <jordan.ellis@vectorcloud.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Procurement Team <procurement@vectorcloud.ai>
Date: Sat, 10 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Renewal + capacity increase request (16 → 32 GPUs)

Hi Ava,

Hope you had a great week. We're coming up on our renewal on May 1 and are evaluating moving from our current 16-GPU reserved pool to a 32-GPU pool for the next 12 months. Growth in inference volume has been faster than we forecasted, and our latency-sensitive endpoints need the additional headroom and KV-cache improvements.

A few asks up front:
- Can you share an updated reserved per-hour rate for 32 GPUs (12-month term) and the incremental discount thresholds?
- Is there a throughput SLO or baseline you can commit to once we double capacity? We currently see occasional token throttling during morning spikes.
- Any recommended change to burst capacity / autoscale settings for predictable cost?

We have procurement and security reviews starting next week. If you can send a proposed SOW + pricing, we can kick off internal approvals.

Thanks,
Jordan
Jordan Ellis
Head of Platform Ops
VectorCloud
[Attachment: vectorcloud_current_usage_q1.csv (csv, 58KB)]

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Ellis <jordan.ellis@vectorcloud.ai>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 10:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal + capacity increase request (16 → 32 GPUs)

Hi Jordan — great to hear from you and congrats on the growth. We can absolutely support a move to a 32-GPU reserved pool for your renewal.

High level:
- I'll attach a proposed SOW and a pricing sheet for a 12-month reserved term here. The sheet includes two pricing bands: a standard reserved per-hour and a committed-throughput tier with tighter per-token economics.
- On throughput SLOs: for a 32-GPU reserved pool we can guarantee 99.5% availability at your current median sequence length and provide a throughput baseline of ~1.8x your current sustained tokens/sec (we'll include the measurement assumptions). For spikes, we recommend enabling a 2-GPU short-burst buffer (billed on usage) to absorb 30–60s surges.
- Autoscale: we suggest a conservative warm-up policy (add 2 GPUs at 65% CPU/GPU mem) with prefetching of hot prefixes — Marcus can add notes on KV-cache sizing.

Next steps:
1) Neha will follow up with the formal pricing sheet and SOW draft.
2) If procurement wants a formal throughput SLA in the contract, let us know the language you need; we can propose SLA metrics and credits.
3) Marcus will validate the workload profile and confirm the burst recommendation.

Attaching: VectorCloud-Renewal-SOW.pdf (draft) and Reserved-Pricing-2027-AVACHEN.xlsx (pricing bands).

Best,
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: VectorCloud-Renewal-SOW.pdf (pdf, 42KB)]
[Attachment: Reserved-Pricing-2027-AVACHEN.xlsx (xlsx, 18KB)]

From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Ellis <jordan.ellis@vectorcloud.ai>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 13 Apr 2027 14:22:00 -07:00
Subject: RE: Renewal + capacity increase request (16 → 32 GPUs) — proposed commercial terms

Jordan — following up with proposed commercial terms for the 12-month reserved pool upgrade. Key items in the attached pricing sheet and SOW: 

Commercial highlights:
- 32-GPU reserved pool, 12-month fixed term.
- Reserved per-hour rate: $14.60 per GPU-hour (applies to core reserved capacity).
- Committed-throughput tier: $13.40 per GPU-hour if you commit to a monthly usage floor equal to 90% of historical average (captures heavy steady-state customers).
- Burst buffer: short-burst GPUs billed at on-demand rate of $3.20 per GPU-hour (metered in 1-minute increments).
- One-time migration assistance credit: $3,500 (applied to month 1 invoice).

SLA and throughput: we propose the following clause (sample):
"Redwood will maintain 99.5% availability for the reserved pool measured on a monthly basis. Throughput baseline will be defined using the baseline profile in Appendix A; credits of 5% of monthly reserved fees will apply for monthly availability below threshold up to three consecutive months."

Notes: Marcus confirmed the KV-cache sizing needed for roughly 30% reduced p99 latency once the pool is resized and warm cache policies are applied. Marcus — can you confirm the engineering notes for Appendix A?

If this looks good, we can:
- Finalize SOW and send to your procurement for redlines.
- Lock pricing for 7 days pending signature to align with your May 1 renewal date.

Thanks — happy to hop on a quick call this week with procurement to walk through the SLA/terms.

Regards,
Neha Kapoor
Senior Account Manager, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: Reserved-Pricing-2027-AVACHEN.xlsx (xlsx, 18KB)]

From: Jordan Ellis <jordan.ellis@vectorcloud.ai>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Procurement Team <procurement@vectorcloud.ai>
Date: Sun, 18 Apr 2027 16:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal + capacity increase request (16 → 32 GPUs)

Thanks Neha — appreciate the clear breakdown. A couple quick clarifications from procurement: 

1) Can the committed-throughput tier be applied retroactively on the invoice if we exceed the usage floor mid-month, or does it require a prior-month commitment? Procurement prefers retroactive true-ups to avoid forecasting risk.
2) We want the burst buffer cap to be configurable; does Redwood allow a monthly cap on burst spend? If so, add that as an Appendix item.
3) The SLA credit (5%) — can we instead get a service credit ladder (5% for one month, 10% if repeated two months) up to a cap of 30%?

If you can confirm answers to 1–3 and send a clean SOW with those potential edits, we can route for signature. We may also ask for a short 30-minute call to align on Appendix A measurements.

Jordan
Jordan Ellis
Head of Platform Ops, VectorCloud

- Reserved per-hour rate: $14.60 per GPU-hour (applies to core reserved capacity).
- Committed-throughput tier: $13.40 per GPU-hour if you commit to a monthly usage floor equal to 90% of historical average (captures heavy steady-state customers).
- Burst buffer: short-burst GPUs billed at on-demand rate of $3.20 per GPU-hour (metered in 1-minute increments).
- One-time migration assistance credit: $3,500 (applied to month 1 invoice).

SLA and throughput: we propose the following clause (sample):
"Redwood will maintain 99.5% availability for the reserved pool measured on a monthly basis. Throughput baseline will be defined using the baseline profile in Appendix A; credits of 5% of monthly reserved fees will apply for monthly availability below threshold up to three consecutive months."

Notes: Marcus confirmed the KV-cache sizing needed for roughly 30% reduced p99 latency once the pool is resized and warm cache policies are applied. Marcus — can you confirm the engineering notes for Appendix A?

If this looks good, we can:
- Finalize SOW and send to your procurement for redlines.
- Lock pricing for 7 days pending signature to align with your May 1 renewal date.

Thanks — happy to hop on a quick call this week with procurement to walk through the SLA/terms.

Regards,
Neha Kapoor
Senior Account Manager, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: Reserved-Pricing-2027-AVACHEN.xlsx (xlsx, 18KB)]

Date: Sun, 18 Apr 2027 16:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal + capacity increase request (16 → 32 GPUs)

Thanks Neha — appreciate the clear breakdown. A couple quick clarifications from procurement: 

1) Can the committed-throughput tier be applied retroactively on the invoice if we exceed the usage floor mid-month, or does it require a prior-month commitment? Procurement prefers retroactive true-ups to avoid forecasting risk.

From: Emily Zhao <emily.zhao@horizon.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 15 Apr 2027 10:08:00 -0700
Subject: MAP + pricing ask for evaluation

Hi Ben,

Thanks for the demo yesterday — the team was impressed with latency and cost projections. We discussed building a short evaluation plan (MAP) that ties product milestones to committed spend so our procurement team can justify an initial commercial commitment.

High-level ask from procurement: can Redwood provide a milestone-based rebate tied to the MAP? Specifically:
- 12-week evaluation -> if Horizon hits milestone A (1M tokens/month), 10% invoice rebate applied;
- Ramp milestone at 6 months to 3M tokens/month -> additional 12% rebate (stacking) if committed ramp schedule met;
- Total potential rebate up to 20% if all MAP milestones achieved and commitments honored.

We need a short doc to show to procurement next week (they want pricing + conditions). Can you share a draft MAP and indicate whether (and at what levels) a commercial rebate is possible? Also please flag any approvals needed on your side.

Thanks,
Emily

emily.zhao@horizon.ai
+1 415-555-0123

Date: Thu, 15 Apr 2027 12:34:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP + pricing ask for evaluation

Emily — really appreciate the clear ask and the context from procurement.

I attached a draft MAP (horizon_MAP_v1.xlsx) and a short commercial sketch. Short answer: we can structure milestone-linked incentives, but anything above a 15% effective rebate requires executive approval due to margin/precedent. Proposed starting point:
- 12-week evaluation (no committed spend, access to hosted API): 8% rebate upon successful validation milestone A.
- 6-month ramp (commit to monthly min tokens): 10% additional rebate if ramp schedule met (combined 18% if both achieved).
- If Horizon signs a 12‑month committed capacity (dedicated/dedicated-reserved) within 90 days, we can add a 2% strategic rebate, bringing the total to 20% — but that needs VP + CFO sign-off.

Reqs/notes:
- Rebates applied as credits on future invoices; not refunds.
- Payment terms: standard net30; finance may require net15 for higher rebate tiers.
- We need SOW + committed ramp schedule (we've put placeholders in the MAP).

I'm looping in Marissa to validate commercial language and to route for internal approvals (Rishi for >15% discount band, Laura for finance sign-off, and Aditya if CFO requests CEO sign-off).

Next steps:
1) Emily — please confirm you can accept credits vs. refunds and confirm the ramp dates.
2) Marissa/Rishi/Finance — internal approval request below.

Cheers,
Ben
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 650-555-0199

Attachments: horizon_MAP_v1.xlsx; Horizon_SOW_draft_v0.2.pdf

Subject: Re: Fwd: MAP + pricing ask for evaluation — request approval (Horizon AI)

Team — reviewed. Finance can approve the 18% credit structure under the following conditions:
1) Credits are applied to future invoices only; no cash refunds.
2) Horizon signs the SOW with the committed ramp schedule and minimum monthly token consumption.
3) If the customer fails to meet ramp milestones but has already received credit, we require an earnback/recapture clause (credit clawback) or an offset on future billing.

title: FastCart: field canonicalization plan + CAIQ delta submission
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morris <elena@fastcart.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina_haddad@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 16 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: FastCart: field canonicalization plan + CAIQ delta submission

Elena —

Thanks again for the walkthrough this week. We did a quick export of the FastCart portal fields and put together an initial mapping to Redwood's CAIQ/CA documentation. See attached field-map-v1.csv (columns: portal_field, suggested_canonical, example_value, notes).

Two asks from our side to move this along:
- Can you confirm the mapped portal_field names for the items under \"Deployment & Network\" and \"Data Retention\"? Our export flagged several variants (e.g., \"retentionPeriod\" vs \"data_retention_days\").
- FastCart limits portal edits to scheduled maintenance windows — can you share the earliest slot we could push the field-name harmonization? We want to align the portal change with our CAIQ delta so the values land in the same weekly snapshot.

Plan I'm proposing (tentative):
1) We canonicalize the field list and push the small renames in the portal (low risk)
2) Mina prepares a CAIQ delta package reflecting the canonical fields + sample artifacts
3) We submit the CAIQ delta (as a single delta commit) on the next allowed window (you mentioned once per week/quarter constraints)

Attaching: field-map-v1.csv (draft) — I zipped a sample CAIQ export for internal review: CAIQ-delta-commit.zip (will follow once we have alignment).

If this sounds OK I can loop in engineering for a short change window doc and propose Monday/Tuesday windows.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter | Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0198

---

Attachment: placeholder-CAIQ-delta-commit.zip (stub)

From: Elena Morris <elena@fastcart.com>

Cc: FastCart Security <security@fastcart.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 16 Apr 2027 11:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: FastCart: field canonicalization plan + CAIQ delta submission

Ben —

Appreciate the clear mapping. Quick notes after a skim of field-map-v1.csv:
- Deployment & Network: our portal exposes two values — \"network_classification\" (internal) and \"exposure_profile\" (customer-visible). For CAIQ purposes, we prefer we map to \"network_classification\" and expose a small computed attribute for downstream queries.
- Data Retention: we currently have both a numeric field (data_retention_days) and a text descriptor (retention_policy_label). The numeric field is canonical internally — the label is derived.

Maintenance windows: we have a short slot on Mon 2027-04-19 01:00–03:00 UTC for low-risk metadata updates (no DB migration). That aligns with your proposed Monday/Tuesday.

One operational constraint: FastCart's security team only accepts CAIQ deltas as a signed bundle via our portal artifact ingest. We can accept a single delta per calendar week; if there's a conflict you may need to queue.

I'll confirm the portal field IDs and attach the raw export by EOD.

Thanks —
Elena Morris
Security Program Manager, FastCart
elena@fastcart.com

---
Attachment: fastcart-portal-export-20270416.json (stub)

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina_haddad@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 19 Apr 2027 08:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: FastCart: field canonicalization plan + CAIQ delta submission

Folks —

Thanks Ben/Elena for the context. Internal ops notes and proposed split of responsibilities:

Summary of constraints:
- FastCart accepts one signed CAIQ delta bundle per week and requires the portal canonical names to be present in the artifact.
- Their maintenance window on Mon 2027-04-19 01:00–03:00 UTC is suitable for metadata-only edits (no downtime expected).

Action plan (recommended):
1) Jordan: coordinate with FastCart to reserve the 01:00–03:00 UTC slot and confirm the portal-field IDs Elena will provide. Please capture their artifact ingest URL and signing requirements. (Owner: Jordan)
2) Mina: take the attached field-map-v1.csv, normalize the header names to CAIQ canonical names, and generate the CAIQ delta bundle (JSON + signature). Target draft by EOD Sun 2027-04-18 so we can review Monday morning. (Owner: Mina)
3) Ben: confirm customer commit and any commercial notes (we may want to note the window in the mutual action plan).

Risk & rollback: because these are metadata updates, rollback is trivial, but we should keep a hashed snapshot and include the pre-change export in the delta bundle.

Also: Mina, please include a short explanatory README inside CAIQ-delta-commit.zip describing field derivations (e.g., why we map retention_policy_label -> data_retention_days).

If we hit the weekly delta limit, plan B is to stage changes in the portal and deliver the signed delta on the next available slot; that's not ideal for the compliance snapshot but workable.

Kimberly Park

kimberly_park@redwood.com

Maintenance windows: we have a short slot on Mon 2027-04-19 01:00–03:00 UTC for low-risk metadata updates (no DB migration). That aligns with your proposed Monday/Tuesday.

One operational constraint: FastCart's security team only accepts CAIQ deltas as a signed bundle via our portal artifact ingest. We can accept a single delta per calendar week; if there's a conflict you may need to queue.

From: Daniel Santos <d.santos@acmehealth.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwoodai.com>
Cc: Procurement <procurement@acmehealth.com>
Date: Thu, 15 Apr 2027 16:12:00 -0700
Subject: Clarify storage of prompts/responses by default (procurement request)

Hi Aisha,

Quick procurement check ahead of our contract review: can you confirm what Redwood stores by default when we make API calls to the hosted inference endpoint?

Specifically:
- Are prompts retained, or are only model responses kept?
- What is the default retention period for request/response logs (prompts, outputs, metadata)?
- Where do those logs live geographically for a US customer — are they stored in a region or multi-region?
- Can we opt out entirely or request on-demand deletion? We need exact language for our security questionnaire and the SOW.

Appreciate a short, precise answer and any supporting artifacts we can attach to our procurement package.

Thanks,
Daniel
Director, IT Security
Acme Health



From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwoodai.com>
To: Daniel Santos <d.santos@acmehealth.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwoodai.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodai.com>
Date: Fri, 16 Apr 2027 09:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Clarify storage of prompts/responses by default (procurement request)

Hi Daniel — thanks for the clear list. Short answers below; attaching a SOC2 summary and a retention matrix that spells out our defaults and options.

- Prompts vs outputs: For Hosted API (Redwood API), prompts (the request payload) and the model outputs (responses) are both considered request/response artifacts and are stored by default. We treat the prompt as part of the request record.

- Default retention period (Hosted): 45 days for request/response artifacts (this includes prompt text, outputs, and standard request metadata such as timestamps and model id). That 45-day period is our default for non-enterprise self-serve customers; it supports debugging, quality monitoring, and safety tooling.

- Residency (Hosted US): Logs are persisted within our US region (primary: us-west-2), and are not replicated to EU/APAC for US-region customers unless configured via contract. We can provide region-specific attestations.

- Opt-outs / deletion: We support on-demand deletion requests via our Data Operations API and manual deletion requests through your account team. For Hosted customers the default is retention → deletion after 45 days; we can process expedited deletion requests (within 72 hours) under agreed workflows. For Dedicated and Private deployments, retention policies are configurable by the customer (see next point).

Attachments sent: SOC2_summary.pdf (attestation excerpt), retention_matrix.csv (breakdown by deployment mode & artifact type).

If you need formal contract language, I can loop in Rishi (security) and Ben (sales ops) to propose SOW wording.

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference

Attachments: SOC2_summary.pdf (stub), retention_matrix.csv (stub)


From: Daniel Santos <d.santos@acmehealth.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwoodai.com>
Cc: Procurement <procurement@acmehealth.com>
Date: Sat, 17 Apr 2027 08:41:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Clarify storage of prompts/responses by default (procurement request)

Thanks — that's helpful. A couple of follow-ups from procurement/security: 

1) We need exact contract language that guarantees US residency for logs and a deletion SLA (ideally 72 hours for expedited deletes). Can that be in the SOW?  
2) Is there a way to prevent Redwood from storing prompts at all (i.e., turn off prompt retention) for hosted customers? If not, is hashing/partial redaction an option so PII isn't stored in plain text?
3) For Dedicated/Private: confirm that retention is customer-configurable and whether Redwood can automate purge on a schedule (e.g., 7 days, 30 days, 90 days) — procurement wants to know whether the purge can be enforced by policy in the stack.

Would prefer proposed SOW language in plain text.

-Daniel


From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwoodai.com>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi_malhotra@redwoodai.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwoodai.com>
Date: Sat, 17 Apr 2027 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Clarify storage of prompts/responses by default (procurement request)

Looping you both in — Acme Health is asking for SOW-friendly language and a deletion SLA. Draft suggested language below that we can use in SOW/contract negotiations. Ben — can you quote a 72-hour expedited deletion SLA as an optional add-on? Rishi — any security redlines we should avoid?

Proposed SOW language (draft):

"Data residency and default retention: For Redwood-hosted API services, Customer data including request payloads (prompts) and model outputs (responses) are retained by Redwood for a standard period of 45 days to support service debugging, safety monitoring, and quality telemetry. For customers requiring specific residency, Redwood will store Customer data in the geographic region specified in the Order Form (for Acme Health: US-West region)."

"Deletion and expedited deletion: Redwood will implement deletion of Customer request/response artifacts in accordance with the Customer's request. Standard deletion following the default retention period occurs automatically after 45 days. Upon receipt of an expedited deletion request, Redwood will confirm receipt within 24 hours and complete deletion from active logs within 72 hours, excluding backups and archival snapshots described in the backup policy (see SOC2_summary.pdf)."

"Dedicated and Private deployments: For Dedicated and Private deployments, retention periods and automatic purge schedules are configurable by the Customer and may be enforced as a policy on the deployment. Redwood will provide automation to support scheduled purges (examples: 7, 30, 90 days) and integration points for Customer key-management and audit logging."  

Other points to consider for Acme procurement:
- Offer optional prompt-hashing or on-the-fly redaction for hosted customers as a paid feature — reduces storing full prompt text.
- Note: backups and archives may be retained longer per backup retention schedule; expedited deletion will remove primary records and cached copies but will follow archival recovery/retention rules in the backup policy.

Attachments: redline_suggestion.docx (draft clause + a small redline)

If that looks good I'll send the draft back to Daniel and include the SOC2 excerpt and retention matrix.

Thanks,
Aisha

--
Aisha Rahman
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Hi Sylvia / Steph,

Thanks again for moving quickly on the private offer. Matrix Health has signed the PO (attached) but they've asked for two operational clarifications before we finalize the private-offer paperwork on AWS:

1) Staged region activation: they want US activation to start immediately when the private offer is accepted (target: 2027-07-01) and EU activation to come online as a second phase (target: 2027-09-01). They'd like invoicing and usage to be tracked separately per region.

2) Tax & billing contact update: Matrix is changing their billing contact and will provide VAT documentation for EU invoices. They need the seller-side invoice address and the billing contact updated on the PO.

Attachments: MatrixHealth_PO_78321.pdf, signed_mou_matrix_health.pdf

Can AWS accept a private-offer amendment that includes staged activation dates (US start / EU delayed start) and region-specific invoicing? If so, what fields do you need from us to generate the private offer amendment (seller account ID, start dates, PO number, billing contact)? Also looping in Marissa (billing) and Jonas (finance) to confirm invoice flow and tax docs.

Next steps I propose:
- Steph: confirm AWS process and required metadata for a 2-phase private offer.
- Marissa: confirm invoices and whether we need separate POs for EU vs US or if AWS can handle single PO with region activation flags.
- Jonas: confirm internal accounting for split regional revenue and VAT handling.

We'd like to have the amendment drafted and the DocuSign link ready by Friday so Matrix can sign and we can push the private offer.

Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com

Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 10:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Private offer — staged region activation + tax and billing update

Hi Ben / Sylvia,

Thanks for the details. AWS supports private offers with configurable start dates per entitlement; we can set region-specific activation windows, but we need the following to draft the amendment:

- Seller account ID (your AWS seller ID)
- Offer ID you want us to amend (if you already have one; if not we can create a new private offer based on the PO)
- Exact start dates per region (UTC)
- Billing contact name, email, and tax ID to appear on invoices
- Clarify whether customer wants separate invoices per region or a single consolidated invoice with line-item breakdowns

From our side that results in either: a) a single private offer with region flags and two activation dates, or b) two linked private offers (one per region) if the customer insists on distinct invoices and start dates. Option (a) is cleaner for the offer lifecycle; option (b) is straightforward for accounting.

If you can provide the seller account ID and confirm the invoice preference I will kick off the contract amendment. Also I can send a preliminary DocuSign envelope for the customer to sign the amendment once it's prepared.

Best,
Steph Miller
AWS Marketplace Partnerships
steph@awsmarketplace.com

---
Note: I will need the customer’s VAT ID for EU invoicing and a signed PO to attach to the amendment.

Date: Tue, 13 Apr 2027 08:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Private offer — staged region activation + tax and billing update

Marissa / Jonas — quick internal note to align on asks from AWS and Matrix:

- Matrix will provide their VAT ID and a new billing contact (see Sylvia’s note below). We need to confirm whether we will:
  * Submit one PO with region activation flags (preferred by AWS), or
  * Create two separate POs/private-offers (one for US, one for EU) to make invoicing explicit.

- Marissa: can you check if our finance/legal prefers separate PO/offer for EU vs US for revenue recognition / VAT? If separate offers are required we should plan the DocuSign envelopes accordingly. Also please prepare the seller account ID and our standard AWS private-offer addendum so Steph can slot details in.

- Jonas: can you confirm whether Redwood can accept split regional invoicing vs consolidated invoices and whether VAT information needs to be attached at acceptance? If there are any accounting constraints that would push us to a particular option, let me know.

Goal: have a recommended approach and DocuSign draft by 2027-04-16 so Matrix can sign and we can request AWS to publish the private-offer amendment.

Ben


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Cc: Steph Miller <steph@awsmarketplace.com>, Sylvia Park <procurement@matrixhealth.com>
Date: Thu, 15 Apr 2027 09:37:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Private offer — staged region activation + tax and billing update

Thanks Ben — quick update from billing side:

- Redwood prefers a single private offer with region activation flags if AWS can issue separate invoices by region (line-items per region). That keeps the contract tidy and avoids duplicate offer management.
- If AWS cannot break out invoices by region, then we will need two linked private offers (US + EU) so Matrix receives EU invoices with VAT applied.

title: Action: small-multiples latency & spend tiles on Console home
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>
Cc: Mike Rhodes <mike.rhodes@acme.com>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Action: small-multiples latency & spend tiles on Console home

Team —

Following the convo with Acme (Mike copied) and a couple of other enterprise prospects, we have a clear ask: customers want an at-a-glance view on Console home that surfaces both latency and spend signals per tenant (or billing tag). The idea is compact \"small-multiples\" tiles — one row per tenant, showing p50/p95 latency sparkline and a spend bar for the selected window (default: 7d). Key goals:

- Detect tenant regressions quickly (spike in p95)
- Correlate latency shifts with spend changes (e.g., sudden burst due to traffic or pricing anomaly)
- Provide quick drill paths: click a tenant -> per-route breakdown, recent errors, and last 24h traces

I attached a quick mock (console-home-tiles-mock.png). High-level product asks:
- Tiles limited to top-20 tenants by spend, with a search to pin any tenant
- 7d default window, 1h aggregation for sparkline; ability to switch to 24h/30d
- Spend attribution: show tokens billed and estimated $ (using current price mapping)
- Toggle to show p50/p95 or both

Questions for engineering/analytics:
1) What rollups do we need (granularity, retention) to support 1h/1m views?
2) Can we derive spend quickly from existing billing events or do we need a new aggregation job?
3) Any perf concerns for a Console home query that may show 20 tenants concurrently?

Requesting: a short feasibility note + rough ETA for an MVP (top-10 tenants, p95 + spend, 7d) by EOD Wednesday 4/14.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Product Manager, Console
Redwood Inference

Attachment: console-home-tiles-mock.png (image/png)


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>, Mike Rhodes <mike.rhodes@acme.com>
Date: Tue, 13 Apr 2027 10:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Action: small-multiples latency & spend tiles on Console home

Ava —

Love this — a few clarifying items from CS/field perspective:

- For \"tenant\" we should default to the billing tenant id that Finance uses (the same tag we surface in invoice exports). If we misalign on tag naming we will confuse accounts.
- Customers will want a link from the tile directly to the current Mutual Action Plan (MAP) or open ticket. Could we have an optional 1-line footnote area for a linked MAP/PS ticket?
- Regarding spend: many customers have committed discounts and multiple price tiers. At minimum we should show raw billed units + estimated list-price $ so sales/customer success have a consistent baseline.

Cc'ing Mike — Mike, would you prefer list-price display or your contracted rate on the tile?

On the engineering asks: I'll defer to Priyom and Dylan, but note that customers expect near-real-time for latency (<=5m) and daily accuracy for spend. If 1m rollups are expensive, we can show p95 as a gateway alert and keep the sparkline at 1h.

- Marissa

From: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 14 Apr 2027 08:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Action: small-multiples latency & spend tiles on Console home

Quick feasibility note below. Short answer: doable but requires a small pipeline + schema additions.

Data assumptions and needs:
- Latency: we currently ingest per-request telemetry with a 1s timestamp; we have hourly and daily rollups but no retained 1m rollups for tenant-level p95 historically. To support 1h sparkline and a 5m near-real-time alert we can:
  - Materialize 5m and 1h rollups keyed by tenant_id + route_id in a new rollup table. 30d retention for 5m, 90d for 1h should be sufficient for Console.
- Spend: billing events exist as line items in billing_events (per-invoice). To show recent spend in near-real-time we need a streaming aggregator that groups billing events by tenant and applies the pricing map. If we show list-price units + estimated list-price $ we can avoid contract-rate lookups at render time.

Suggested MVP scope:
- Build 1h rollups for top-20 tenants (weekly job to recompute top-20) + realtime windowed aggregator to keep a 5m p95 for only top-10 tenants.
- Expose an API endpoint /api/console/tiles/tenants that returns cached JSON per-home-view (recompute cache every 1m for top tenants).

Effort rough estimate:
- Rollup pipelines (ETL + infra): 2 engineers x 2 weeks
- API + cache: 1 engineer x 1 week
- Frontend tiles + drill UX: 1 FE engineer x 1 week (MVP limited visuals)
- Backfill scripts and testing: 1 engineer x 4 days

Storage/cost impact: expect additional storage for 5m rollups ~50GB/month for global tenant set; limiting to top-20 reduces that by ~10x. Also small incremental compute for streaming aggregator.

I attached a sample query that would power the API (cost-latency-rollup-proposal.sql).

Priyom
Senior Data Engineer, Platform Metrics
Redwood Inference

>

> - Regarding spend: many customers have committed discounts and multiple price tiers. At minimum we should show raw billed units + estimated list-price $ so sales/customer success have a consistent baseline.
> 
> Cc'ing Mike — Mike, would you prefer list-price display or your contracted rate on the tile?
> 
> On the engineering asks: I'll defer to Priyom and Dylan, but note that customers expect near-real-time for latency (<=5m) and daily accuracy for spend. If 1m rollups are expensive, we can show p95 as a gateway alert and keep the sparkline at 1h.
> 
> - Marissa


From: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 15 Apr 2027 11:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Action: small-multiples latency & spend tiles on Console home

Putting on my infra hat — a couple of notes and a recommended constrained MVP: 

- Live compute: computing 5m p95 for *all* tenants at 1m cadence is expensive. We should scope an MVP to top-10 tenants and provide a soft-fail for "others" (show last computed value + link to per-tenant page for on-demand compute).
- Cache strategy: use a read-through cache with 1m TTL for top tenants; fall back to on-demand worker for pinned tenants. This will keep Console home QPS affordable.
- Backfill: if we want historical sparklines beyond the cache window we need a backfill job; estimate 3 days effort to backfill 90d for top-20 using the offline rollups.

MVP timeline (assuming Priyom's estimates):
- Week 1: ETL + small rollup infra + API scaffolding
- Week 2: FE tiles + API integration + cache tuning
- Week 3: testing, backfill and polish, docs

If everyone is aligned I can spin up the cache and allocate infra quota for a 2-week sprint.

- Dylan

To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 15 Apr 2027 11:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Action: small-multiples latency & spend tiles on Console home

Putting on my infra hat — a couple of notes and a recommended constrained MVP: 

- Live compute: computing 5m p95 for *all* tenants at 1m cadence is expensive. We should scope an MVP to top-10 tenants and provide a soft-fail for "others" (show last computed value + link to per-tenant page for on-demand compute).
- Cache strategy: use a read-through cache with 1m TTL for top tenants; fall back to on-demand worker for pinned tenants. This will keep Console home QPS affordable.
- Backfill: if we want historical sparklines beyond the cache window we need a backfill job; estimate 3 days effort to backfill 90d for top-20 using the offline rollups.

MVP timeline (assuming Priyom's estimates):
- Week 1: ETL + small rollup infra + API scaffolding
- Week 2: FE tiles + API integration + cache tuning
- Week 3: testing, backfill and polish, docs

If everyone is aligned I can spin up the cache and allocate infra quota for a 2-week sprint.

- Dylan

On Wed, Apr 14, 2027 at 08:45 AM Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Quick feasibility note below. Short answer: doable but requires a small pipeline + schema additions.
> 
> Data assumptions and needs:
> - Latency: we currently ingest per-request telemetry with a 1s timestamp; we have hourly and daily rollups but no retained 1m rollups for tenant-level p95 historically. To support 1h sparkline and a 5m near-real-time alert we can:
>   - Materialize 5m and 1h rollups keyed by tenant_id + route_id in a new rollup table. 30d retention for 5m, 90d for 1h should be sufficient for Console.
> - Spend: billing events exist as line items in billing_events (per-invoice). To show recent spend in near-real-time we need a streaming aggregator that groups billing events by tenant and applies the pricing map. If we show list-price units + estimated list-price $ we can avoid contract-rate lookups at render time.

Subject: Clarify platform fee amortization + support tiers for VPC

Hi Aditya,

Thanks again for yesterday's demo — the team was impressed with the latency numbers and the routing options. Before we move to formal SOW language, procurement needs a clearer breakdown of how Redwood treats the platform fee vs. licensing and support. Specifically:

- Is the platform fee a one-time upfront license, an annual charge, or amortized monthly?
- For on-prem/VPC installs, do you offer a per-seat vs. per-token meter? Which is recommended for our usage profile (50k inference requests/day, 8k monthly active users)?
- Support tiers: can you confirm response times for P1/P2, and whether 24x7 on-call is included at the Standard vs. Premium support level?
- Any standard migration credits or one-time onboarding discounts for 2-year committed deals?

I attached our RFP excerpt and our SOC2 snapshot. If you can send a short SKU/line-item example we can drop into procurement for budget approval, that would help us move fast.

Best,
Jordan
Procurement Manager, Acme Health

Attachments: ACME_RFP_v2.pdf, SOC2-Attestation.pdf

From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Kim <jordan.kim@acmehealth.com>
Cc: Priya Singh <priya.singh@acmehealth.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 13 Apr 2027 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Clarify platform fee amortization + support tiers for VPC

Jordan — thanks for the clear list. Quick answers and an attached example line-item table below. TL;DR: platform fee is an annual subscription that can be amortized monthly; licensing for VPC is primarily capacity-committed (GPU hours) with optional seat-based admin licenses.

Short answers: 
- Platform fee: annual, invoiced up-front or spread monthly on invoice (we can accommodate customer accounting preferences). For a 2-year commitment we typically offer to credit 25% of year-1 platform fee pro-rated into year-2 (applied as a one-time migration credit).
- Licensing: we offer (A) committed capacity (preferred for predictable throughput) priced as vGPU-hour blocks, or (B) metered seat + token mix for variable workloads. For your profile we recommend committed capacity with a lightweight token meter for burst (saves ~18–25% vs pure metered).
- Support tiers: Standard = business hours with 4-hr P1 SLAs and next-business-day P2; Premium = 24x7 on-call with 1-hr P1 and 4-hr P2. Premium also includes quarterly architecture reviews and 24/7 escalation channel.
- Migration/onboarding credits: Standard practice is a one-time onboarding credit (15% of year-1 platform fee) tied to a 24-month commitment; we can move to 20% for deals > $1M ARR.

Attached is an example pricing breakdown (line items + sample invoice phrasing). Highlights:
- Platform Fee (Annual): $150,000
- Committed Capacity (vGPU): $0.95 / vGPU-hour (24/7 reserved) — estimated for your baseline: $360,000 / year
- Admin Seats (optional): $120 / seat / month (10 seats assumed)
- Premium Support (optional): $45,000 / year
- One-time migration credit: 15% of Platform Fee ($22,500) applied on first invoice for 24-month commit

If you want, Marissa can put together an alternative that replaces part of the committed capacity with a lower-commit burst pool to reduce upfront cost. Sean, can you confirm whether the proposed migration credit sits inside our standard discount band?

Best,
Aditya
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Attachment: Redwood_LineItem_Example.xlsx

title: Proposal: staged reserve windows + hot-standby routing credits
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Tara Singh <tara.singh@orionhealth.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-04-11T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Staged reserve windows + hot-standby routing credits — proposal overview

Hi Tara,

Thanks for the time yesterday. Per our discussion, attached is a focused proposal that addresses Orion Health's need for predictable committed throughput across two regions (us-west-2, us-east-1) while minimizing duplicate reserved spend during the initial ramp. High-level asks you mentioned were:

- Separate committed pools in each region with per-pool throughput guarantees
- A way to avoid paying full replication fees during the first 6–12 months of ramp
- Cross-region routing for DR with clear unit economics (credits/fees)

Attachments: redwood-dedicated-reserve-v2.xlsx (pricing sheet), orion-map-routing-sketch.png (topology sketch)

Proposed pattern (summary):
1) Staged reserve windows: commit 60% upfront across both regions, with an optional top-off window at 6 months to reach 100%. Pricing shows stepped discounts for the top-off.
2) Hot-standby credit: while second-region capacity remains passive, we offer a reduced replication credit (applied as routing credits) that covers cross-region failover traffic up to an agreed monthly token threshold.
3) Route-by-policy: requests normally route to region-local pools; on failover we route to the standby pool with the credit covering incremental cost up to threshold; above threshold the standard cross-region throughput rate applies.

If this aligns with your procurement band's constraints, we'll produce an updated SOW and a DocuSign-ready term with the staged top-off option baked in.

Would you like a quick call tomorrow to walk the numbers with your finance/procurement team?

Best,
Ben
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference


From: Tara Singh <tara.singh@orionhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-04-12T08:40:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Staged reserve windows + hot-standby routing credits — proposal overview

Ben —

Thanks for the clear summary and the attachments. A few clarifying questions from our side before we pull in procurement:

1) How do you model the \"hot-standby\" credit if we do a failover test that lasts an entire weekend? Is the credit applied daily or monthly?
2) For the staged top-off, are there penalties if we choose not to exercise the 6-month top-off? Procurement is worried about being locked into unused commits.
3) Can you confirm that the standby pool will not count against our committed capacity for usage-based overage billing?

Also, procurement will want the numerical example showing blended unit cost across months during the stage-up (not just the percent discounts). Can you drop in a 9-month ramp example?

Thanks — can we do a 30-minute call Thursday at 11:00 PT?

Best,
Tara
Tara Singh
Head of Cloud Architecture
Orion Health


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: 2027-04-12T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Staged reserve windows + hot-standby routing credits — prep for Orion call

Team —

Tara asked for a few specifics. I'm proposing we prepare:

- A 9-month blended-cost spreadsheet (monthly committed draw + routing credit consumption scenarios)
- Clear language for the SOW about \"no penalty if top-off is not exercised\" but top-off pricing disappears after the 6-month window (i.e., option expires).
- Definition of \"failover test\" and the billing treatment: propose credit is applied pro rata daily but usage computed monthly; tests under 72 hours fall under credit budget.

Vivek — can you produce the blended example (3 scenarios: nominal, weekend failover once/month, sustained failover <=72 hours)?

Subject: Staged reserve windows + hot-standby routing credits — proposal overview

Hi Tara,

Thanks for the time yesterday. Per our discussion, attached is a focused proposal that addresses Orion Health's need for predictable committed throughput across two regions (us-west-2, us-east-1) while minimizing duplicate reserved spend during the initial ramp. High-level asks you mentioned were:

- Separate committed pools in each region with per-pool throughput guarantees
- A way to avoid paying full replication fees during the first 6–12 months of ramp
- Cross-region routing for DR with clear unit economics (credits/fees)

Attachments: redwood-dedicated-reserve-v2.xlsx (pricing sheet), orion-map-routing-sketch.png (topology sketch)

Proposed pattern (summary):
1) Staged reserve windows: commit 60% upfront across both regions, with an optional top-off window at 6 months to reach 100%. Pricing shows stepped discounts for the top-off.
2) Hot-standby credit: while second-region capacity remains passive, we offer a reduced replication credit (applied as routing credits) that covers cross-region failover traffic up to an agreed monthly token threshold.
3) Route-by-policy: requests normally route to region-local pools; on failover we route to the standby pool with the credit covering incremental cost up to threshold; above threshold the standard cross-region throughput rate applies.

If this aligns with your procurement band's constraints, we'll produce an updated SOW and a DocuSign-ready term with the staged top-off option baked in.

Would you like a quick call tomorrow to walk the numbers with your finance/procurement team?

Ben

title: Anchored breakpoints & graduated rebate proposal for EzPay - hosted API
preview:
From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.inference>
To: Emily Chen <emily@ezpay.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.inference>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.inference>
Date: Fri, 10 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Anchored breakpoints & graduated rebate proposal for EzPay

Hi Emily —

Thanks again for the conversation yesterday. As discussed, I put together a lightweight proposal that tries to balance EzPay's ask for predictability and your desire to capture upside. The attached PDF (EZPay_pricing_proposal_v1.pdf) contains the full table; below is the executive summary and a suggested mutual action plan.

Executive summary
- Baseline per-token list rate: $0.00045 (hosted API, text-generation)
- Anchored breakpoints (quarterly aggregated):
  * Tier A: 0 - 50M tokens -> baseline rate
  * Tier B: 50M - 200M tokens -> marginal rebate 12%
  * Tier C: 200M+ tokens -> marginal rebate 22%
- Transitional smoothing credit: for Q1 post-commit, we apply a retroactive blended credit up to 6% if you ramp slower than forecast (protects you vs. step cliffs)
- Overage treatment: marginal overage rebate capped at 15% (to avoid runaway discounts)

Why this structure? It preserves marginal economics (you only get the higher rebate for incremental tokens beyond a breakpoint) while adding an anchored smoothing credit so billing is readable for your finance team. This is a middle ground between pure blended and pure marginal approaches.

Next steps / asks
1) Internal confirmation: can procurement accept retroactive credits applied as invoice adjustments?
2) Would EzPay prefer rebates applied retroactively (credit back on invoice) or as upfront monthly bill-credit?
3) Can we aim for an LOI signature within the next two weeks to lock pricing for calendar Q3?

Proposed mutual action plan (high-level)
- Week 0: Aisha shares v1 proposal (attached)
- Week 1: Procurement review & confirm billing approach; Redwood legal to prepare LOI
- Week 2: Sign LOI; schedule technical kickoff

Attaching: EZPay_pricing_proposal_v1.pdf with the breakpoint tables and examples. Happy to hop on a short call to walk through the math.

Best,

Aisha Rahman
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
<aisha@redwood.inference>
+1 (415) 555-0142

Attachments: EZPay_pricing_proposal_v1.pdf


From: Emily Chen <emily@ezpay.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.inference>
Cc: EzPay Procurement <procurement@ezpay.com>
Date: Sat, 11 Apr 2027 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Anchored breakpoints & graduated rebate proposal for EzPay

Aisha — thanks for the quick turn. I read the proposal and the structure makes sense in principle but our finance team has a few practical asks before we can move forward: 

1) Simplicity: our billing ops prefers either a simple blended effective rate applied monthly or a very clearly documented marginal process. The mix with a transitional smoothing credit is new to them — can you show a sample invoice and two worked examples (one slow ramp, one fast ramp)?
2) Overage concern: if we spike in usage for a short period (e.g., product launch), will we be penalized by lower rebates vs. a blended model?
3) Commitment term: is the proposal tied to a 12-month commitment? What flexibility do we have to re-open thresholds mid-term if market/usage changes?

Also looping in procurement@ezpay.com for contract/PO questions. If you can share a one-page summary and the sample invoice examples, that would help speed our internal review.

Best,
Emily Chen
Head of Platform, EzPay
emily@ezpay.com

On Fri, Apr 10, 2027 at 09:12 AM Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.inference> wrote:
> (original message omitted)


From: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.inference>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.inference>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.inference>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.inference>
Date: Sat, 11 Apr 2027 14:22:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Anchored breakpoints & graduated rebate proposal for EzPay

Folks — forwarding Emily's reply (excerpted below). Procurement is worried about complexity and overage spikes. Can we propose a defensible bridging approach that keeps margin integrity for Redwood but addresses their "simplicity" ask?

Key asks from Emily/procurement:
- Provide two worked invoice examples (slow ramp vs fast ramp)
- Clarify how overage spikes are treated vs blended alternatives
- Confirm term flexibility (can they re-open thresholds if usage materially deviates)

My suggested options to evaluate: 
A) Retroactive marginal with a 90-day smoothing bridge: rebates applied retroactively but we provide a bill credit smoothing buffer that attenuates a step cliff in the first quarter.
B) Hybrid monthly blended floor + marginal upside: apply a floor blended rate for monthly billing and then true-up quarterly with marginal rebates (this makes monthly invoices predictable).
C) Simplified blended option: offer a blended effective rate at an agreed forecast with a runway for re-negotiation at 6 months. Lower upside but simplest for ops.

Please review and annotate in-line. I want to get back to Emily with a clear recommendation and sample numbers by EOD Monday.

Thanks,
Aisha


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.inference>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha@redwood.inference>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.inference>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.inference>
Date: Mon, 12 Apr 2027 08:47:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Fwd: Anchored breakpoints & graduated rebate proposal for EzPay

Agree with the three-option breakdown. My recommendation (and the one I think minimizes ops friction) is Option B with a small tweak: quarterly true-up combined with a one-time ramp credit that phases out after Q1. Practical details below and I attached a working model (pricing_model_sheet.xlsx).

Recommended approach (Option B, detailed)
- Monthly invoice: apply a predictable floor blended rate equal to a conservative forecasted effective rate (e.g., $0.00042/token for Q1).
- Quarterly true-up: after quarter close, compute actual marginal rebates retroactively and apply as an invoice credit. This preserves marginal economics while keeping monthly invoices simple.
- One-time ramp credit: if actual Q1 usage < forecast by >20%, apply a smoothing credit up to 6% of billed amount (not to exceed $50k) to avoid an early churn risk.
- Overage: treat as incremental tokens above the top tier; rebate applied at marginal rates but capped at 15% (we can negotiate higher cap for higher commits).
- Term flexibility: 12-month initial commitment with a mid-term review at 6 months; thresholds can be adjusted by mutual consent if usage deviates >40% vs forecast.

Sample numbers (see attached workbook for math)
- Forecast: 120M tokens / quarter
- Floor blended monthly: $0.00042/token -> monthly invoice simplicity
- Quarterly retroactive calc: if actual tokens hit tier C, effective retroactive rebate increases effective rate by ~10-15% on marginal tokens

Impacts to Redwood: minor complexity in running quarterly true-ups (we already do this for several book-of-business deals). Finance ops: we should document the crediting mechanism clearly (invoice line item: "Quarterly rebate adjustment - EZPay").

If you want, I can draft the one-page summary (blurb + two examples) and Aisha can send it to Emily. Recommend we include the workbook as a supplement.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Director, Strategic Accounts
ben@redwood.inference

Attachments: pricing_model_sheet.xlsx

Agreed approach (bridged ladder)
- Monthly billing: a predictable floor blended rate (we propose $0.00042/token for Q1) so your monthly invoices are simple for ops.
- Quarterly true-up: after quarter close we compute marginal rebates retroactively and apply as an invoice credit (preserves marginal economics).
