Marcus/Marissa — looping you in to confirm internal owners and earliest availability.

Camila Reyes
Partner Success Manager, Redwood Inference
camila_reyes@redwood.com


From: Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwood.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 02 Apr 2027 10:23:00 -0700
Subject: Internal: ProcurePlus — MSA redlines + PO field netting (notes + ask)

Folks — internal notes after Rajiv's message (see pasted below). Key asks: legal to prioritize redline triage; integrations to confirm field mappings for Ariba vs Coupa and whether we can do automated netting. We should avoid blocking the vendor activation over non-critical language.

Priority items I see:
- Payment netting: ProcurePlus wants the ability to net invoices with credit memos. Our current AR pipeline prefers distinct invoices + credits; netting is supported but requires the finance team to enable automated offsetting and we need PO-level references to ensure matching. Marissa — can our Ariba connector carry a "netReference" field or a combined "applyToInvoiceId"? If not, we'll propose using "vendorReference" + line-level accounting to reconcile.
- PO fields: ProcurePlus requires billTo (org id), costCenter, accountCode. In Coupa we typically map 'supplier_billing_org' -> billTo and 'account_code' -> accountCode. For Ariba we use custom XML tags; need to confirm exact tag names.
- Legal redlines: Marcus — please flag any absolutely blocking clauses. The usual suspects: data retention/backup, liability cap, indemnity carve-outs, audit access. If we can offer a limited audit window and narrow retention to operational logs, we may keep procurement happy without changing the cap.

Proposed timeline: legal redline summary by EOD Mon (Apr 4); integration confirms field map by Tue (Apr 5); vendor activation scheduled for week of Apr 12 assuming sign-off.

Attachments: (we'll attach draft redline summary and a sample PO field map)

--Original message pasted--


From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 03 Apr 2027 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Internal: ProcurePlus — MSA redlines + PO field netting (notes + ask)

Camila — thanks for the summary. Quick legal decision notes:

1) Liability cap — keep at standard multiple of fees but add mutuality for gross negligence only. Procurement may push; we should offer a paragraph clarifying consequential damages carve-out.
2) Audit & logs — we will accept a scoped audit clause limited to 1 inspection/year and pre-notification; deny broad on-site rights. Offer to provide audit logs exports for specified date ranges instead.
3) Data retention — propose 90 days for transient logs and 12 months for invoice/transaction data; this is reasonable for procurement.

Netting: legal is comfortable as long as netting/credit math is auditable and there's an explicit clause obligating both parties to keep reconciled records. If finance requires additional contractual language we can add a short section under "Payment; Invoicing".

I'll prepare a redline patch (MSA_Redline_patch.diff) with the three high-priority edits. We can push the rest into the order form (payment terms, PO field obligations). Legal can turn around this patch by EOD Mon (Apr 4) if nothing else unexpected appears.

Marcus Lin
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference
marcus_lin@redwood.com

Please review and let us know any must-have edits; legal can do a 48-hour turnaround after you respond.

title: Request: Sponsorship for trial surge + combined discount/credit approval - Lighthouse Labs
preview:
From: Emma Diaz <emma@lighthouse.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
Cc: procure@lighthouse.ai
Date: Sat, 03 Apr 2027 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Request: Sponsorship for trial surge + discount

Hi Aditya,

Thanks again for the quick demo last week — the team at Lighthouse Labs is excited about how Redwood could speed up our inferencing pipeline. We kicked off an internal pilot and hit a usage spike this month that pushed us past the free trial cap. Procurement is asking for a combined ask: some emergency credits to keep the pilot live plus a short-term commercial discount if we sign to a 12-month commitment.

Quick numbers:
- Estimated extra consumption this month: ~450k tokens (spike tied to a new customer beta)
- Pilot -> Expected production ARR if we convert: $250k ARR conservatively
- Procurement timeline: we can sign MSAs within ~4 weeks if terms are nailed down

What we're requesting:
1) A one-time sponsorship credit to cover the current surge (~$10k–$25k depending on how you size credits) to avoid throttling the pilot.
2) A promotional discount (they are asking ~30% off list) applied to the first 12 months tied to a signed commitment.

I attached a short proposal and an export of usage for the last six weeks. Legal already flagged standard terms but procurement is willing to accept a shorter review window if your team can provide a clean term-sheet.

Can we escalate for exec approval? This is timing sensitive for the signed commitment.

Best,
Emma
Emma Diaz
Head of Product, Lighthouse Labs
-- attachments: sponsorship_proposal.pdf; lighthouse_usage_export.csv

From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 03 Apr 2027 10:02:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Request: Sponsorship for trial surge + discount

Marissa, Ben — forwarding Lighthouse's ask. TL;DR: potential $250k ARR if they convert; pilot hit a usage surge and procurement wants credits + a 12-month promotional discount. Attachments from them included (see below).

My recommendation (from SDR/AE view):
- Offer a $20k one-time sponsorship credit to cover the immediate spike (covers current overage + buffer).
- Offer a 25% promotional discount on list pricing for the first 12 months in exchange for a signed 12-month commitment and a short NRR check at 6 months.
- Require Legal review and inclusion of a usage-based cap clause (to avoid open-ended subsidized overages).

Rationale: Lighthouse is engineering-led, has strong technical fit, and is already integrating our SDK in two internal services — high chance of expansion across their product lines. If we decline or move slowly, they will evaluate other vendors next week.

Attachments: redwood_term_sheet_v2.docx (draft term summary I can push to procurement once you greenlight).

Ask: need exec approval for the combined credit + discount (this is outside standard field authority). If you want different numbers, I can adjust.

—Aditya

---- Original message ----
"From: Emma Diaz <emma@lighthouse.ai>
Subject: Request: Sponsorship for trial surge + discount
Date: Sat, 03 Apr 2027 09:15:00 -0700
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
Cc: procure@lighthouse.ai

Hi Aditya,
... (original message attached)"

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 04 Apr 2027 08:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Request: Sponsorship for trial surge + discount

Aditya — appreciate the context. Quick thoughts:
- $20k credit + 25% feels slightly generous given risk of sustained overuse; propose we tighten to $15k credit + 20% discount for 12 months.
- Alternative: keep $20k credit but require a 12-month commit + a 6-month review gate tied to specific usage milestones. If they miss the milestone, discount steps down.
- Need Finance to pre-authorize applying credits (I can loop Mary in after Marissa signs off).

If Marissa is comfortable with your recommendation we'll route to legal + finance for the final paperwork.

Ben
Director, Strategic Accounts
Redwood Inference

On Sat, Apr 3, 2027 at 10:02 AM Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com> wrote:
> Marissa, Ben — forwarding Lighthouse's ask. TL;DR: potential $250k ARR if they convert; pilot hit a usage surge and procurement wants credits + a 12-month promotional discount.
> ...

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 05 Apr 2027 14:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Request: Sponsorship for trial surge + discount (EXEC APPROVAL)

Thanks team — I reviewed the ask and the usage export. We should move quickly but with guardrails. Exec decision: APPROVE the combined concession with conditions.

Approved terms (conditional):
- One-time sponsorship credit: $20,000 applied to current overage and first invoice.
- Promotional discount: 25% off list for the first 12 months, contingent on a signed 12-month commitment and a minimum commitment value of $150k ARR.
- Require a 6-month usage & business review: if Lighthouse does not meet the agreed usage milestones, the discount will step down to 10% for the remainder of the term.
- No change to SLAs or data residency commitments. Legal to prepare an addendum to the MSA; Finance to approve credit accounting and set supplier code for tracking.

Ben — please loop Mary (Finance) and Legal (contracts@redwood.com) and get me the updated term-sheet. Aditya — coordinate with Lighthouse to get signature within 3 weeks; if they ask for steeper discounts, escalate to me with clear ARR growth projections.

Marissa Cole
VP Sales, Redwood Inference

Subject: Re: Fwd: Request: Sponsorship for trial surge + discount

title: Request: On-site Private Deploy Readiness & Architecture Walkthrough
preview:
From: Samir Rao <samir@lumenworks.com>
To: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-04-03T16:12:00-07:00
Subject: Private infra review — need architecture deep-dive for on-prem pilot

Hi Avery,

Thanks again for the demo last month — LumenWorks is ready to take the next step and we need a focused architecture deep-dive to lock down the on-prem / VPC design and air-gapped constraints for our pilot. We need to validate:

- network segmentation (no outbound egress except approved update proxies)
- key management options (KMS integration & HSM requirements)
- allowed maintenance windows (we run batch jobs overnight)
- storage encryption and audit log retention policies

Can your team run a 60–90 minute session next week to walk our infra and surface any blocking items? People who should attend from our side: myself (platform lead) and Priya Khatri (security lead). We are in PST and prefer mornings. Suggested slots: Tue 4/6 09:00–10:30, Wed 4/7 10:00–11:30, or Thu 4/8 08:30–10:00.

If helpful, I can send our current network diagram and a short list of non-negotiable constraints.

Appreciate it — Samir

Samir Rao
Platform Lead, LumenWorks
samir@lumenworks.com
+1 (415) 555-0192


From: Avery Johnson <avery.johnson@redwood.com>
To: Samir Rao <samir@lumenworks.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: 2027-04-03T17:04:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Private infra review — need architecture deep-dive for on-prem pilot

Hi Samir — great to hear you're ready to move forward. Thanks for the initial constraints list; a focused 75-minute session should surface any blockers.

We can cover network topology, KMS/HSM integration patterns, requirements for air-gapped update flows, and the ops playbook for maintenance windows. Suggested internal attendees: Ben Carter (SE) and Monica Patel (Solutions PM) — I copied Ben now and will add Monica on the follow-up.

From the slots you suggested, Tue 4/6 09:00–10:30 PST works for Ben and me. Please confirm if that’s still OK for you and Priya.

Prework: I attached a one-page prework checklist that helps us prioritize topics and collect any diagrams or inventories we should review ahead of time. If you can send your network diagram and any restricted vendor lists before EOD Monday 4/5, Diego (our on-prem architect) can do a quick pass.

Attachment: redwood_private_prework.pdf (1 page)

Thanks — Avery

Avery Johnson
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
avery.johnson@redwood.com

Two asks from our side to make the session high value:
1) If you can share an IP/topology diagram (even a hand-drawn PNG) it saves 20 minutes of back-and-forth.
2) Let us know whether patching must be manual (air-gapped) or can be proxied through a staging update host — that determines whether we bring the update-agent design or we discuss fully offline patch workflows.

Also, Tue 4/6 09:00 PST is fine, but if Priya needs a later slot we have a backup at Wed 4/7 10:30–12:00.

I’ll briefly present our recommended airtight patterns and then hand to Diego for the on-prem specifics (hardware/network constraints).

Ben Carter

title: Post-call recap & who pulls the switch — org roles + procurement path
preview:
From: Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Ethan Roberts <ethan@novarx.com>, procurement@novarx.com
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 1 Apr 2027 16:12:00 -0700
Subject: Post-call recap & quick asks (Redwood pilot + decision criteria)

Ethan — thanks again for the 30m earlier. Quick recap and three asks so we can line up next steps: 

Call highlights (short):
- Use case: inference at scale for batch and low-latency clinical summarization.
- Decision criteria you called out: latency at 95th, per-inference cost, data residency/SOC2, and rollback SLA.
- Pilot ask: 2-week evaluation (100k tokens/day burst) to validate latency & cost.

Asks / next steps:
1) Could you share the org chart or point person who \"pulls the switch\" on pilots (economic buyer / sponsor)?
2) Security/compliance owner for SOC2 evidence and any specific data residency constraints.
3) Procurement contact and preferred contract path (MSA + PO vs. procurement portal).

I attached my call notes (call-notes-20270401.txt) and a one-pager for the pilot. If you prefer, an intro to the sponsor + security owner works great — will keep it light. 

Proposed next step: 15–20m sync with the economic buyer + security owner next week to confirm criteria and timeline. Available Tue/Wed mornings PT.

Thanks —
Camila
Camila Reyes | Account Executive, Redwood Inference
camila_reyes@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0118

Attachments: call-notes-20270401.txt, redwood-pilot-one-pager.pdf


From: Ethan Roberts <ethan@novarx.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila_reyes@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@novarx.com
Date: Thu, 1 Apr 2027 17:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Post-call recap & quick asks (Redwood pilot + decision criteria)

Camila — thanks for the summary and the materials. Quick responses: 

- Economic buyer / sponsor is Lena Ortiz, VP Product (lena.ortiz@novarx.com). She owns pilot approvals and budget.

title: Dedicated capacity: traffic taxonomy & sizing questionnaire
preview:
From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Sophie Martin <sophie.martin@nimbusretail.com>, Raj Patel <raj@nimbusretail.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 1 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Dedicated capacity: traffic taxonomy & sizing questionnaire

Hi Sophie / Raj —

Thanks again for taking the time this week. Attached is a short pre-sales sizing questionnaire we use to scope Dedicated deployments. To make the initial sizing accurate for Nimbus Retail we mostly need:

- Peak and sustained concurrency (sessions/minute and p95 sessions)
- Prompt length distribution (percentiles for 1–50, 51–200, 201–1000 tokens)
- Response token averages and p95 token lengths (different for short chat vs. long generative flows)
- Session shape (average session duration, % multi-turn conversations, KV cache hit assumptions)
- Burst windows (typical duration, recurrence, and peak multiplier vs baseline)
- SLA targets (p50/p95 latency targets, required tail SLOs)
- Any multimodal items or embedding-heavy endpoints (frequency and size)

If you can attach a 24–72h sample of traffic (CSV with timestamp, endpoint, prompt_tokens, response_tokens, session_id) that would be ideal — we can run a quick token-flow calculator and propose a GPU mix (SKUs, quantization suggestions, and initial burst pool sizing).

We usually turn these around in 48 hours once we have the sample. I put a ~48-hour window in the questionnaire but tell me a date that works for you.

Attachments: pre-sales-sizing-questionnaire.pdf (short form), example CSV header.

Next steps: once we have the sample I’ll loop in Marissa (SE) to build an initial sizing and proposed pilot plan.

Thanks,

Camila Reyes
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
camila.reyes@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0182

---
Redwood Inference — production-first LLM inference (hosted, dedicated, private)

From: Sophie Martin <sophie.martin@nimbusretail.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
Cc: Raj Patel <raj@nimbusretail.com>
Date: Thu, 1 Apr 2027 11:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity: traffic taxonomy & sizing questionnaire

Hi Camila —

Thanks, this is helpful. I attached a small 48-hour sample (nimbus_traffic_sample.csv) from our staging environment and a quick answer sheet below — happy to expand if you need more detail.

Inline answers:
- Peak concurrency (sessions active at once): roughly 1,100 concurrent sessions in peak 10-min windows; sustained baseline ~250.
- Prompt length distribution (approx): 40% <= 50 tokens, 45% 51–200 tokens, 15% 201–800 tokens (long queues / order generation).
- Response tokens (averages): chat flows ~60 tokens avg, long generative flows ~320 tokens avg; p95 for long flows ~720 tokens.
- Session shape: average session 3.2 turns, KV cache effectiveness unknown today (we do have conversation IDs), estimated 70% of traffic is conversational with reuse.
- Burst patterns: heavy retail promotion bursts 11:00–11:20 PT and 18:30–18:50 PT local time, multipliers ~4–5x baseline for those windows; also weekend afternoon spikes.
- SLA: for core chat features we want p95 <= 400ms (token streaming OK), for long generation p95 <= 2.5s is acceptable.
- Multimodal: currently none; embeddings used for similarity searches are a separate job and not in this sample.

We can grant a short-lived link to a larger sample if needed — Raj will send that if you want a 7-day dump.

Files attached: nimbus_traffic_sample.csv (columns: ts, endpoint, prompt_tokens, response_tokens, session_id, user_region)

Let me know if you want me to fill anything in the PDF (we left a couple of fields blank around fallback rules and retention).

Best,

Sophie Martin
Head of AI Platform, Nimbus Retail
Sophie.Martin@nimbusretail.com

From: Sophie Martin <sophie.martin@nimbusretail.com>
Date: Thu, Apr 1, 2027 at 11:05 AM
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity: traffic taxonomy & sizing questionnaire
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
Cc: Raj Patel <raj@nimbusretail.com>

(See attached CSV and inline answers)

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sophie Martin <sophie.martin@nimbusretail.com>
Date: Thu, 1 Apr 2027 14:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Dedicated capacity: traffic taxonomy & sizing questionnaire

Thanks Camila — reviewed the CSV quickly. High level thoughts (initial, conservative sizing):

- Traffic shape: % long responses and 4–5x microbursts drives need for warm burst capacity (we should avoid cold-start model loads during those 10–20 minute windows).
- Pilot cluster: target an A800/TPU-equivalent pool with mixed instance type: primary rtx-a800-heavy pool for long-generation and a smaller A100-style pool for low-latency chat.
- Quantization: test 4-bit quantization for the long-gen model to reduce memory and fit more replicas; keep a 16-bit fallback for chat-critical routes to meet p95 latency.
- KV cache: enable session-level K/V caching; with 70% conversational reuse we should see 20–35% token cost reduction. However, cache eviction and affinity will matter — recommend session-affinity sticky routing for pilot.
- Suggested sizing (pilot): baseline capacity to handle sustained baseline (~250 concurrent) + headroom for 4x multiplier = plan for ~1,000–1,200 concurrent capacity. For bursts, provide an elastic burst pool sized for an additional 800–1,200 concurrent sessions with fast pre-warm.
- Fallback policy: route to smaller quantized model when A800 pool exceeds 90% occupancy — provide a graceful quality fallback and rate-limit non-critical endpoints.

I attached a quick spreadsheet with the token-flow calc and SKU mapping (initial-sizing-estimate.xlsx).

Immediate ask for Sophie: can you confirm retention/PII constraints for the telemetry (we anonymized session IDs but would prefer explicit confirmation)? Also confirm whether embeddings pipeline will run on the same cluster during peak windows — the CSV indicates embeddings are separate, but confirm.

Happy to hop on a 30-min technical call with your infra/ML engineer team to walk through the draft.

Marissa Cole
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai | +1 650-555-0191

From: Sophie Martin <sophie.martin@nimbusretail.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Raj Patel <raj@nimbusretail.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 3 Apr 2027 15:48:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity: traffic taxonomy & sizing questionnaire

Hi Marissa, Camila —

Thanks for the quick review and suggested approach. That sizing direction aligns with our internal expectations. A few quick confirmations:

- Retention/PII: we confirm the staging CSV is anonymized and acceptable for pre-sales analysis; production telemetry will have stricter retention (30 days) but not needed for sizing.
- Embeddings: embeddings jobs are scheduled off-peak to a different cluster — they will not contend during peak customer-facing bursts.
- Pilot timing: we can do a pilot week starting Apr 19th (two-week window would be even better).

We also created a temporary S3 bucket with a 7-day sample if you want to run more exhaustive sims: https://s3.nimbus.internal/sample-dumps/20270401-7d/ (short-lived token). Raj will DM creds.

Please let us know the next steps and any calendar times for a technical sync (we can do Apr 5th or Apr 6th mornings PT).

From: Alejandra Ruiz <alejandra@searchlytics.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.ai>
Cc: 
Date: Fri, 02 Apr 2027 09:15:00 -0000
Subject: Embed onboarding: priming, index backfill and rerank QA questions

Hi Camila —

Thanks for taking this. We are kicking off our internal project to replace keyword search with an embeddings + rerank pipeline. A few items to surface before the kickoff so we can scope the work: 

- We currently have ~12M product descriptions stored in a single Postgres table (export attached: searchlytics_current_index.csv).
- Our current vector vendor is VectorCloud (managed). Wem evaluating whether to keep them or move to Pinecone/Redis.
- Main asks: recommended "cold-start" priming strategy, expected cost/CPU profile for a 2M doc backfill, and a short set of rerank eval criteria (precision at top-5 / relevance labeling guidance).

Can you share a short mutual-action plan and a list of prep items we should have ready for a smooth handoff to implementation/CS? Were hoping for a 4-6 week milestone plan that preserves production latency SLAs.

Also copied procurement at VectorCloud since they asked about indexing cadence and snapshot exports.

Best,
Alejandra Ruiz
Head of Search Engineering
Searchlytics

Attachments: searchlytics_current_index.csv (CSV, 12MB approx)


From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.ai>
To: Alejandra Ruiz <alejandra@searchlytics.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 02 Apr 2027 11:02:00 -0000
Subject: Re: Embed onboarding: priming, index backfill and rerank QA questions

Hi Alejandra —

Great details, thanks for the context and the export. Quick responses + proposed next steps below. Im copying Ben (CS lead) and Grace (implementation/ops) to stamp on the execution plan.

High-level answers:
1) Cold-start priming: we recommend a two-phase priming approach — (A) seed priming with high-quality canonical documents (1-2k examples) covering top query intents, (B) staged prefix priming using the first 100k doc backfill wave to warm the KV cache. This keeps early latency bounded while avoiding an expensive full repopulate.

2) Backfill sizing: for a 2M doc wave with 1536-d embeddings, expect roughly 1.2-1.6TB of vector storage uncompressed; with 8-bit quantization that drops significantly. We'll include cost estimates by vendor during the kickoff.

3) Rerank evals: start with a 500-query holdout set and annotate top-10 candidates for NDCG@10 and Precision@5. We'll provide a labeling rubric (intent, relevance tier, explanation).

Proposed mutual action plan (short):
- Week 0 (prep): provide sample query logs (top 2k queries), confirm preferred vector store, and export mapping of primary keys.
- Week 1-2 (seed priming & smoke): seed dataset ingest, seed embeddings, rerank model fine-tune candidates + smoke tests against a small subset.
- Week 3-4 (wave 1 backfill): first 200k doc wave with monitoring and rerank calibration.
- Week 5-6 (scale & cutover): remaining waves + acceptance testing, rollback playbook ready.

Questions for you before kickoff:
- Which vector store (VectorCloud, Pinecone, Redis, self-hosted) do you plan to run for production?
- Any compliance constraints on encryption at rest that would require a private deployment?
- Do you have an existing labeling resource or should we provide a small rapid-labeling engagement?

I attached our onboarding template (redwood_onboard_template.pdf) which includes artifacts we typically ask for. If this looks good I propose a 30-minute kickoff early next week to align owners and sign the SOW.

Thanks,
Camila Reyes
Customer Solutions Lead, Redwood Inference
camila@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0192

Attachments: redwood_onboard_template.pdf (PDF, 420KB)


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.ai>
Cc: Alejandra Ruiz <alejandra@searchlytics.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 02 Apr 2027 13:10:00 -0000
Subject: Re: Embed onboarding: priming, index backfill and rerank QA questions

Quick notes from CS on execution strategy:

- Backfill approach: incremental waves (200k -> 500k -> 1M+) with a hot-prefix cache. We prefer prioritizing documents tied to top revenue categories first to accelerate user-visible improvement.
- Snapshot + delta ingestion: ask VectorCloud for daily snapshot exports (compressed) and use deltas for ongoing sync to keep bandwidth minimal.
- Rerank thresholds: require P@5 >= 0.62 on holdout and NDCG@10 uplift >= 0.08 vs current baseline before canary cutover. Were flexible on exact numbers but those are sensible targets based on similar accounts.
- Rollback playbook: maintain a short window with automatic fallback to old search for 30 minutes post-cutover.

I'll own the acceptance criteria and the rerank QA rubric; Grace will run implementation check-ins and staging validation.

Ben Carter
Senior CSM, Redwood Inference
Attachments: none

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Lauren Crisp <lauren.crisp@acmehealth.com>; NetworkOps Team <netops@acmehealth.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 1 Apr 2027 09:15:00 +0000
Subject: Private deployment acceptance kickoff — networking, KMS, and evidence Q&A

Hi Lauren / NetworkOps,

Thanks again for choosing Redwood for the private deployment. Before we schedule the operational kick-off, I wanted to consolidate a short acceptance checklist and a couple of specific questions so we can avoid back-and-forth during the handoff. I attached a one-page checklist (redwood-private-onboard-checklist-v1.xlsx) and a short KMS options summary (kms-integration-options.pdf).

Top asks from our side:
- Confirm VPC peering/private interconnect window (proposed: 2027-04-12 02:00–04:00 UTC). Will that maintenance window work?
- KMS: do you prefer customer-managed keys in Cloud KMS or an on-prem HSM? If HSM, what vendor/connector do we need to support (YubiHSM, Thales)?
- Routing: will outbound egress be SNAT through a single NAT gateway, or will you allow our service subnet direct egress? We need an allowlist for egress IPs if you SNAT.
- Evidence: your security team asked for examples of audit logs and token-level access logs. We can share a redacted sample if that helps.

If we can get answers to the above, we’ll run the preflight smoke tests and handover playbook in the next sprint. Proposed next steps:
1) Lauren confirms the maintenance window and KMS preference.
2) NetworkOps shares the CIDR for peering and the egress plan.
3) We schedule a 60-min kickoff with infra and security (I’ll invite Neha and Alex).

Thanks — I’ll follow up with a calendar invite after I hear from you.

Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0199

Attachments: redwood-private-onboard-checklist-v1.xlsx (spreadsheet), kms-integration-options.pdf (doc)

> - Confirm VPC peering/private interconnect window (proposed: 2027-04-12 02:00–04:00 UTC). Will that maintenance window work?
> - KMS: do you prefer customer-managed keys in Cloud KMS or an on-prem HSM? If HSM, what vendor/connector do we need to support (YubiHSM, Thales)?
> - Routing: will outbound egress be SNAT through a single NAT gateway, or will you allow our service subnet direct egress? We need an allowlist for egress IPs if you SNAT.
> - Evidence: your security team asked for examples of audit logs and token-level access logs. We can share a redacted sample if that helps.
> 
> If we can get answers to the above, we’ll run the preflight smoke tests and handover playbook in the next sprint. Proposed next steps:
> 1) Lauren confirms the maintenance window and KMS preference.
> 2) NetworkOps shares the CIDR for peering and the egress plan.
> 3) We schedule a 60-min kickoff with infra and security (I’ll invite Neha and Alex).

> Ben Carter

Date: Fri, 2 Apr 2027 08:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Kickstart: Routing playbook & scale-test session — initial prework

Ava / team —

Putting together a short model matrix to bring to the session. Attached is a draft shortlist (model_shortlist.xlsx) with recommended default fallback order and quantization options. High level suggestions:

- Tier 1 (low-latency, higher cost): Redwood-optimized gpt-4o-small-quant — good for critical endpoints with strict latency SLAs.
- Tier 2 (balanced): vetted open LLM v1.3 (8-bit) — good quality/cost tradeoff for general traffic.
- Tier 3 (economic): Claude-lite / instruction-tuned small models — best for background and batch tasks.

Fallback policy (proposed defaults)
- If Tier 1 latency >= 200ms P50 or a 5% error spike, failover to Tier 2 for 60s with circuit-breaker backoff.
- Region mapping: prefer region nearest to customer; if capacity constrained, route to secondary region within same GDPR boundary.

Rohan — can you check capacity and whether we can reserve a small dedicated pool for the dry-run on Apr 7? Connor, Evelyn — please flag any models you think shouldn't be on the shortlist.

Marcus
Lead, Inference Ops
marcus.lin@redwood.com

Date: Thu, 1 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Stacked demo/on-call window — propose compact group slots for multiple prospects

Hi all —

Thanks for your interest in a short, focused walkthrough of Redwood Inference. We have a few teams that asked for 30-minute demos in the same week; to be efficient were proposing a stacked window where we run three 25-min live demos back-to-back (20 min demo + 5 min quick Q). That keeps each session compact and gives teams flexibility to join the slot that fits their calendar.

Proposed windows (all times PDT):
- Option A: Tue Apr 6 — 09:00–10:15 (three 25-min slots starting on the hour)
- Option B: Wed Apr 7 — 10:00–11:15
- Option C: Thu Apr 8 — 16:00–17:15

If youre up for this format, reply with the single slot your team prefers (e.g., Tue slot 1) and we'll send a dedicated calendar invite + short agenda. Well keep the material tight: serving runtime highlights, cost/latency tradeoffs, and a quick Q/A tailored to your use case.

title: Request: one-time pricing holdback for Cloudstream launch delay
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Maya Srinivasan <maya_srinivasan@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 1 Apr 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Request: one-time pricing holdback for Cloudstream launch delay

Team —

Quick background: Cloudstream is targeting a May 10 public launch for a major feature that depends on our multi-model routing (MMR) to orchestrate embeddings + retrieval + generator pipelines. We certificated a compatibility shim but the product team needs a few weeks beyond their original timeline to deliver the native MMR support (DEPLOY-834). Cloudstream is telling us the launch cannot proceed without the native behavior because performance/latency regressions with the shim break UX.

They are asking for a short commercial remedy so they can keep pricing/promotional commitments with their customers. Requested: a one-time pricing holdback (50% off hosted inference for 45 days post-launch) + a $25k one-time credit to bridge ARR recognition timing.

I attached a short commercial_summary.pdf and a draft term sheet. My ask: can we approve a conditional, timeboxed remedy that ties the concession to a product milestone (native MMR in prod by May 31) and to a usage cap?

Key constraints:
- Finance will need a clear dollar cap and accounting language (I included a draft in the term sheet).
- Product (Marcus) — can you confirm the May 31 target and whether a limited scope release (AP-surface only) would satisfy Cloudstream?
- CS (Maya) — what's the customer risk (churn / legal escalation) if we don't offer any remedy?

Proposed quick decision path: Karthik signs off on exec-level exception with the above conditions, I convey to Cloudstream and attach DS link for approval.

Ben
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0182

Attachments: commercial_summary.pdf, draft_term_sheet.docx


From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Maya Srinivasan <maya_srinivasan@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 1 Apr 2027 09:35:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: one-time pricing holdback for Cloudstream launch delay

Ben —

Thanks for the context and the clear ask. Short answers up front: we are targeting to have native MMR in staging by May 15 and GA by May 31 in the us-west-2 region. The remaining work is around routing policy stability and KV-cache eviction edge cases under high-concurrency scenarios.

We can commit to the May 31 GA date subject to the following guardrails:
- The release will be scoped to our generator 2.1+ path and the routing for embeddings->retrieval->generator for sequences up to 1k tokens initially.
- We will provide documented fallbacks and a migration runbook for Cloudstream.
- If any critical regressions surface in the 48 hours after GA, we will trigger a rollback window and the concession will be voided unless we deliver the fix within 7 days.

If Karthik and finance agree to a usage cap and a timebound credit, product is comfortable supporting this as an exec-approved commercial remedy. Marcus

Marcus Lin
VP, Product — Serving & Runtime
marcus_lin@redwood.com
Attachment: product_timeline.xlsx


From: Maya Srinivasan <maya_srinivasan@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 1 Apr 2027 11:08:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: one-time pricing holdback for Cloudstream launch delay

Ben —

Customer perspective: Cloudstream has escalated to their CFO and product VP. If we do nothing they will accelerate conversations with two competitors who already have a multi-model orchestration story. Risk: conversion impact on their early enterprise pipeline and a potential pause on the pilot that would delay our FY27 revenue recognition.

CS recommendation: accept a conditional remedy to preserve the relationship and lock in a short-term commitment from Cloudstream (6 month minimum commit post-launch) + an explicit joint migration plan. We should require:
- A usage cap (suggestion: $150k billable tokens equivalent over 45 days)
- A written sign-off on the migration runbook and a shared rollback SLA

I can own the migration onboarding and the post-launch health checks.

— Maya
Maya Srinivasan
Director, Customer Success
maya_srinivasan@redwood.com


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Maya Srinivasan <maya_srinivasan@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 1 Apr 2027 12:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: one-time pricing holdback for Cloudstream launch delay

All —

I support a timeboxed, conditional commercial remedy with the conditions proposed. My decision principles: preserve revenue pipeline without setting an open precedent, and make any concession contingent on product deliverables and a clear cap. Final decision here:

Approve a one-time concession composed of:
- $25,000 one-time credit (applied up front against invoicing)
- 45 days of 50% off hosted inference up to a usage cap of $150,000 equivalent (after which normal rates apply)
- Concession void if native MMR GA is not in prod by 2027-05-31 or if a critical regression triggers the rollback condition Marcus outlined
- Customer must sign the attached draft_term_sheet.docx and commit to a minimum 6-month post-launch usage agreement to be eligible

Ben, please get Cloudstream to sign the term sheet and send me the DocuSign link. Marcus and Maya — please coordinate on the migration runbook and the monitoring plan; I want a short exec note on the plan by EOD tomorrow so I can record the approval and align finance.

Karthik
Karthik Iyer
SVP, Global Sales
karthik_iyer@redwood.com


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Jake Miller <jake@cloudstream.com>, Anita Rao <anita@cloudstream.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>, Maya Srinivasan <maya_srinivasan@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 2 Apr 2027 15:08:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Request: one-time pricing holdback for Cloudstream launch delay

Jake / Anita —

Thanks for the call this morning. Redwood is prepared to offer a short, conditional commercial remedy to keep your launch on schedule while we finalize native multi-model routing. Summary of the approved package (subject to the attached term sheet and signatures):

- $25,000 one-time credit applied at invoice issuance
- 45 days of 50% hosted inference pricing, capped at $150,000 equivalent usage
- Cloudstream must sign the attached term sheet and agree to a 6-month minimum billing commitment post-launch
- This remedy is contingent on Redwood delivering native MMR to production by 2027-05-31 and the migration runbook acceptance; if GA is missed or a critical regression causes a rollback, the concession will be voided

Full term sheet and DocuSign link (for signature) attached. Ben

Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.com
Attachments: draft_term_sheet.docx

title: Invoice ingestion: portal preferred + email fallback / onboarding form update
preview:
From: Marco Ruiz <marco@acmesupply.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 29 Mar 2027 16:02:00 -0700
Subject: Onboarding + invoice submission for ACME Supply

Hi Camila,

Thanks for the quick intro last week. We are ready to start sending invoices to Redwood for POs issued earlier this quarter. A couple quick questions before we go live:

1) Is the vendor portal mandatory for invoices, or can we email invoices to invoices@redwood.com?
2) If email is acceptable as a fallback, do you have a required subject line or filename schema we should follow?
3) Are there any recent changes to the onboarding form (tax, remittance, W-9, bank details) we should be aware of?

I attached our standard invoice template to show the fields we usually provide.

Attachment: sample_invoice_template.xlsx (stub)

Best,
Marco Ruiz
AP Manager, ACME Supply
marco@acmesupply.com
+1 415-555-0192


From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.com>
To: Marco Ruiz <marco@acmesupply.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 30 Mar 2027 09:24:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Onboarding + invoice submission for ACME Supply

Hi Marco — great to connect.

Short answers: portal preferred; email allowed as fallback with strict conventions; onboarding form updated (see link). Details below so you can circulate internally.

Portal (preferred)
- URL: https://invoices.redwood.com/vendor-portal
- Benefits: automated PO matching, immediate validation (missing PO, invalid tax ID), and faster payment runs.
- File types accepted: PDF (preferred), CSV (line-level), or XLSX (if you include a single invoice per workbook).

Email fallback (only when portal is not available)
- Send to: invoices@redwood.com AND copy AP mailbox: ap-team@redwood.com
- Required subject prefix: [REDFWD-INVOICE] followed by PO# (e.g., [REDFWD-INVOICE] PO123456)
- Filename convention: REDWOOD_PO123456_INV<your-invoice-number>.pdf (no spaces)
- Minimum fields in body or attached PDF: Invoice number, invoice date, PO number, line items (description, qty, unit price), total, remit-to name/address, vendor tax ID/EIN.

New onboarding form (action required)
- We just released version 2 of the vendor onboarding form. It now requires: remittance address, vendor legal name (exact as on W-9), 1099 classification, EFT/ACH boolean, and tax ID type.
- Please complete: https://drive.google.com/open?id=onboarding-form-2027

PO stamping and payment terms
- PO stamping: Redwood requires either a stamped invoice (we stamp on receipt) or a clear PO reference in subject and on the invoice. If the PO is missing we will hold for manual review.
- Payment terms remain Net 45 for first 6 months for new vendors; after verification we can update to contractual terms.

Attachments: I attached the vendor onboarding form and a short sample CSV row you can use to validate column mapping. 

Attachment: vendor_onboarding_form_v2.pdf (stub)

Next steps:
1) Please complete the onboarding form (link above).
2) If you choose email fallback, mirror the subject/filename conventions exactly.
3) If you want SFTP ingestion for high-volume batches, let me know; I will loop in our integrations team.

Cc'ing Ben from Finance to confirm payment workflow.

Thanks,
Camila Reyes
Procurement Program Manager | Redwood Inference
camila.reyes@redwood.com


From: Marco Ruiz <marco@acmesupply.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.com>
Cc: ACME Supply AP <invoices@acmesupply.com>
Date: Thu, 1 Apr 2027 08:11:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Onboarding + invoice submission for ACME Supply

Thanks Camila — that's helpful. A couple follow-ups from our AP team:

- SFTP: we'd prefer SFTP for daily batches. Is there a provisioning lead?
- CSV schema: can you confirm the exact column order for CSV ingest (we want to avoid repeated rejects)?
- If portal is down, is it sufficient to put PO number only in subject and filename, or do you require the PO to be a separate visible field in the PDF?

I've attached a single-row CSV (sample_invoice_row.csv) showing our production export. If you could flag any column mismatches we will update the feed before going live.

Attachment: sample_invoice_row.csv (stub)

Best,
Marco


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Marco Ruiz <marco@acmesupply.com>
Cc: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 2 Apr 2027 11:48:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Onboarding + invoice submission for ACME Supply

Hi Marco — Ben here (Finance). Quick confirmations so you can proceed:

1) SFTP: we can provision a vendor SFTP. Camila, please send the onboarding checklist to the integrations team (PROC-412) and start the onboarding task.
2) CSV schema: we validate on these columns (exact order): invoice_number, invoice_date (YYYY-MM-DD), po_number, line_description, qty, unit_price, line_total, invoice_total, remit_name, remit_address_line1, remit_city, remit_state, remit_zip, vendor_tax_id. If any fields are omitted the portal will flag the invoice and create a manual ticket.
3) Email fallback: subject prefix + PO in filename is acceptable, but PO must also be present inside the invoice PDF (top-right header or on the first page). If missing, we will hold the invoice and open a ticket which delays payment.

Action for ACME:
- Complete vendor_onboarding_form_v2.pdf and attach W-9 (or local tax equivalent).
- Confirm whether you prefer SFTP or portal; if SFTP, Marco, send an example daily file (we got the sample CSV) and we will provision a user.

Priya (Finance Ops) is looped for vendor activation and ACH setup. Priya — please confirm the Net 45 provisional terms and advise on any exceptions for recurring suppliers.

Regards,
Ben Carter
Director, Accounts Payable | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
+1 650-555-0147

1) SFTP: we can provision a vendor SFTP. Camila, please send the onboarding checklist to the integrations team (PROC-412) and start the onboarding task.
2) CSV schema: we validate on these columns (exact order): invoice_number, invoice_date (YYYY-MM-DD), po_number, line_description, qty, unit_price, line_total, invoice_total, remit_name, remit_address_line1, remit_city, remit_state, remit_zip, vendor_tax_id. If any fields are omitted the portal will flag the invoice and create a manual ticket.
3) Email fallback: subject prefix + PO in filename is acceptable, but PO must also be present inside the invoice PDF (top-right header or on the first page). If missing, we will hold the invoice and open a ticket which delays payment.
